content.text:
1. User Authentication & Security (Foundation)
Description:
Implements a secure, local-only authentication system for the three user roles (Passenger, Dispatcher, Administrator) without external dependencies.
Goal:
Establish a secure entry point that enforces password standards and role-based access control (RBAC).
Requirements:
Login: Username and password verification.
Password Policy: Minimum 8 characters.
Security: Store passwords using salted hashing (e.g., BCrypt).
RBAC: Define roles for Passenger, Dispatcher, and Admin to restrict API access.
Data Privacy: Message desensitization logic based on sensitivity levels.
Technical Notes:
Framework: Spring Security (Spring Boot).
Database: PostgreSQL (User table).
Environment: Offline LAN.
Expected Output:
A login screen and a backend service that issues JWTs or session tokens and validates password complexity.
2. Smart Search System (Passenger Feature)
Description:
A high-performance search interface for passengers to find routes and stops using various input methods.
Goal:
Provide a fast, "fuzzy" search experience that prioritizes relevant results.
Requirements:
Search Types: Route number, stop name, or keywords.
Smart Input: Support for Autocomplete and Pinyin/initial letter matching (e.g., "bj" for "Beijing").
Sorting Logic: Results must be sorted by "Frequency Priority + Stop Popularity."
Deduplication: Automatically remove duplicate stop entries from results.
Technical Notes:
Frontend: Angular (English interface).
Backend: Spring Boot REST API.
State Management: RxJS for handling search debounce and stream handling.
Expected Output:
A search bar that provides real-time suggestions and a results list ordered by the specified priority algorithm.
3. Notification Preferences & DND (Passenger Feature)
Description:
Allows passengers to customize how and when they receive bus-related alerts.
Goal:
Give users control over their notification experience to avoid "alert fatigue."
Requirements:
Preferences: Toggle switches for arrival reminders and successful reservations.
DND (Do Not Disturb): Users can set time ranges (e.g., 22:00ΓÇô07:00) during which notifications are silenced.
Reminder Logic: Default arrival reminder set to 10 minutes before the bus arrives.
Technical Notes:
Frontend: Angular Reactive Forms.
State Management: Save preferences to the PostgreSQL user profile.
Expected Output:
A settings page with toggles and time-pickers that correctly saves and applies user-defined notification rules.
4. Dispatcher Workflow & Approval Engine (Operations)
Description:
A task-oriented dashboard for dispatchers to manage route changes and data approvals.
Goal:
Automate the operational workflow with visual tracking and escalation.
Requirements:
Workflow Types: Route data changes, reminder configurations, abnormal data reviews.
Logic: Support for conditional branching, joint/parallel approvals, and "Return to Submitter."
Timeout Alerts: Tasks unprocessed for 24 hours must trigger an escalation warning.
Features: Batch processing of tasks and a visual progress tracker (stepper component).
Technical Notes:
State Management: Workflow state machine in Spring Boot.
UI: Angular Material for the dashboard and task lists.
Expected Output:
A dashboard where dispatchers can view, approve, or reject tasks with visible progress indicators.
5. Unified Message Center (Shared Feature)
Description:
A centralized hub where users can view all notifications, reminders, and system alerts.
Goal:
Ensure users never miss critical information, even if they were offline or in DND mode.
Requirements:
Message Types: Reservation success, arrival reminders, and "Missed Check-in" (triggered 5 minutes after start time).
Management: Mark as read/unread, delete, and filter by type.
Desensitization: Mask sensitive information (e.g., partial phone numbers or IDs) in the UI.
Technical Notes:
Backend: Internal Message Queue (no external SMS/Push).
Task Scheduling: Spring @Scheduled tasks to trigger reminders.
Expected Output:
An "Inbox" style interface that updates in real-time (via polling or WebSockets) with system-generated messages.
6. Data Integration & Cleaning Engine (System/Admin)
Description:
The backend pipeline that ingests, parses, and cleans external bus data files.
Goal:
Standardize inconsistent raw data into a clean, structured format for the database.
Requirements:
Parsing: Support structured HTML and JSON templates.
Field Mapping: Map raw fields to standard stops, addresses, and apartment types.
Cleaning Rules:
Unify areas to "πÄí".
Unify prices to "yuan/month".
Handle missing values: Mark as "NULL" and log the source.
Audit Trail: Retain logs of all cleaning actions and versioning for stop structure changes.
Technical Notes:
Backend: Spring Batch or custom parser service.
Storage: PostgreSQL with field-level versioning.
Expected Output:
A robust data import service that handles messy input and populates the database with standardized records.
7. Administrator Control Panel (Admin Feature)
Description:
A management interface for platform-wide settings and dictionaries.
Goal:
Provide administrators with the tools to fine-tune the platform's behavior without code changes.
Requirements:
Templates: Maintain and edit notification message templates.
Rule Weights: Adjust the numerical weights for the "Frequency + Popularity" search algorithm.
Dictionaries: Manage standard field dictionaries (e.g., stop types, status codes).
Technical Notes:
Frontend: Angular CRUD tables.
API: RESTful endpoints with Admin-only access.
Expected Output:
A suite of configuration pages that update system-wide constants and rules in real-time.
8. Observability & Health Monitoring (DevOps)
Description:
Internal monitoring tools to ensure platform stability in an offline environment.
Goal:
Detect performance bottlenecks and system failures automatically.
Requirements:
Logging: Structured logging for all key workflows (search, parsing, queue).
Tracing: Assign Trace IDs to follow a request from search to queue consumption.
Health Checks: API endpoints to monitor database and service health.
Alerting: Trigger local alerts/diagnostic reports if:
Message queue backlogs exceed a threshold.
API P95 response time > 500ms.
Technical Notes:
Library: Spring Boot Actuator and Micrometer.
Strategy: Local backup strategy for PostgreSQL.
Expected Output:
A system health dashboard (or log-based reporting) that notifies admins of performance degradation. # Original Prompt

Develop a "City Bus Operation and Service Coordination Platform" targeting three user roles: passengers, dispatchers, and operation administrators. 

Passengers access an English interface built with Angular, where they can search for bus routes and stops by route number, stop name, or keywords. The search supports autocomplete suggestions and pinyin/initial letter matching, with results sorted by "frequency priority + stop popularity" and automatically deduplicated. The search results page allows users to set notification preferences for arrival reminders, including toggle switches and "do not disturb" periods (e.g., no notifications from 22:00 to 07:00). Notifications for events such as successful reservations, upcoming reminders (default 10 minutes in advance), and missed check-ins (5 minutes after start time) are centralized in a message center for unified viewing. 

Dispatchers use a task dashboard to handle tasks such as "route data change approvals, reminder rule configurations, and abnormal data reviews." The workflow engine supports conditional branching, joint/parallel approvals, task returns for resubmission, and timeout alerts (tasks unprocessed for 24 hours trigger escalation warnings). It also provides visual progress tracking and batch processing capabilities. 

Administrators maintain notification templates, sorting rule weights, and field standard dictionaries. 

On the system side, Spring Boot is deployed in an offline local area network, with RESTful APIs decoupled from the frontend. All data is stored in PostgreSQL with a local backup strategy. Bus data integration supports structured parsing of HTML/JSON templates, with field mapping and version management to track changes in stop structures. Fields such as stop names, addresses, residential area names, apartment types, areas, and prices undergo standardized cleaning and missing value handling (e.g., areas unified as πÄí, prices unified as yuan/month, missing fields marked as NULL with source logging). Cleaning rules are configurable, with audit logs retained. 

User authentication is limited to username and password verification locally, requiring passwords of at least 8 characters, stored with salted hashing. Message content is desensitized based on sensitivity levels. Notifications are uniformly written to an in-platform message queue and triggered by local scheduled tasks, without reliance on external SMS/email/push services. 

Observability features include structured logging, metrics, and health checks. Key workflows (search, parsing, process flows, queue consumption) are tracked with trace IDs. Queue backlogs and API P95 response times exceeding 500ms trigger local alerts and diagnostic reports. ### Overall Judgment on Whether the Tests Are Sufficient to Catch the Vast Majority of Problems

- Conclusion: `Fail`
- Reason (Judgment Boundary): The current tests do cover some important foundations, especially RBAC basics, workflow ownership, search ranking/dedup happy paths, DND logic, JSON-cleaning happy path, and selected API semantics. However, they do not cover enough of the highest-risk business paths to catch the vast majority of serious defects. Missing areas include message-center ownership, notification preference controller validation, workflow mode semantics and automatic escalation, import HTML/audit/versioning, admin runtime template/dictionary usage, observability alert triggering, frontend behavior, and multiple edge/boundary cases. Because of those gaps, the project could still have severe defects while the provided test suite appears healthy.
- Minimal improvement suggestions by priority:
  1. Add ownership tests for message-center read/delete and add automatic workflow-escalation tests.
  2. Add integration tests for notification preferences, search API, admin template application, and import HTML/audit/versioning.
  3. Add observability alert-trigger tests and a small frontend test suite for login, route gating, and core screens.
  4. Add boundary tests for empty results, time windows, duplicate actions, and malformed inputs.

## Final Acceptance Summary

- The project is substantial and clearly more than a demo. It contains a real full-stack structure, Angular UI, Spring Boot backend, PostgreSQL schema, queueing, admin screens, workflow features, and observability.
- The acceptance result is not a full pass because several prompt-critical behaviors are only partially realized:
  - imported/admin-managed data does not power the passenger search experience
  - workflow timeout warnings are manual instead of automatic
  - admin-maintained templates/dictionaries do not drive runtime behavior
  - sensitivity-level masking is separated from the real inbox pipeline
  - test coverage is not broad enough for the main risk areas
- Acceptance verdict: `Partially Pass` check .tmp/self-test_report.md and fill potential gaps

<content>1: # Delivery Acceptance / Project Architecture Review
2: 
3: Project: `/Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91`
4: Review date: 2026-03-25
5: Scope reviewed: `/Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack`
6: Overall judgment: `Partially Pass`
7: 
8: ## Verification Summary
9: 
10: - Frontend build was actually executed and succeeded with `cd /Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/frontend && npm install && npm run build`. The configured build entry and output path are defined in `frontend/package.json:5-8` and `frontend/angular.json:11-15`. The generated files exist under `/Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/frontend/dist/mindflow-auth-frontend`.
11: - Backend runtime and Spring test execution were not executable in this environment because `java` and `mvn` are not installed here. This is an environment restriction, not a project defect. The backend entry and tests are statically confirmable from `backend/Dockerfile:1-20`, `backend/pom.xml:1-79`, `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:27-33`, and `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh:172-284`.
12: - Docker was not started because the inspection instructions explicitly said not to start Docker-related commands. The documented full-stack path remains reproducible locally from `README.md:9-19`, `docker-compose.yml:1-49`, and `.env.example:1-17`.
13: 
14: ## Prioritized Issues
15: 
16: ### High
17: 
18: 1. Imported/admin-managed data is not connected to passenger search, so the search experience runs on seeded sample data instead of the integration pipeline output.
19:   - Evidence: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/search/SearchService.java:167-170`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/search/TransitStopBootstrapService.java:18-33`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/integration/DataImportService.java:165-179`
20:   - Impact: Core business data changes made by admins/import jobs do not propagate to the passenger-facing search feature, weakening the prompt's "bus data integration + version management + search" story.
21:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Persist validated import output into `transit_stops` or a canonical stop catalog and rebuild search from that canonical source instead of the bootstrap sample set.
22: 2. Workflow timeout escalation warnings are manual, not automatic after 24 hours as required.
23:   - Evidence: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowController.java:85-89`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowService.java:107-120`, `README.md:63-64`
24:   - Impact: The delivered workflow engine does not autonomously raise timeout alerts; operators only get escalation if someone manually triggers the evaluation endpoint or clicks the UI button.
25:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add a scheduled job that evaluates stale workflow tasks and writes an alert/diagnostic record without manual intervention.
26: 
27: ### Medium
28: 
29: 1. Notification templates and field dictionaries are CRUD-only; runtime flows do not consume them.
30:   - Evidence: template CRUD in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/admin/AdminControlService.java:67-95`; hardcoded notification message bodies in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:37-45`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:66-71`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:83-87`; hardcoded import field mapping in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/integration/DataImportService.java:153-157`; dictionary CRUD only in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/admin/AdminControlService.java:98-129`
31:   - Impact: Admin edits do not materially change notification generation or field mapping behavior, so the "maintain templates / dictionaries" capability is only partially operational.
32:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Resolve notification titles/bodies from `notification_templates` and use `field_dictionaries` during import field normalization/mapping.
33: 2. Sensitivity-level desensitization is not integrated into the real message-center pipeline.
34:   - Evidence: real inbox records have no sensitivity field in `backend/src/main/resources/schema.sql:36-57`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageEntity.java:23-46`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageQueueEventEntity.java:23-44`; queue masking is generic regex masking in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageQueueService.java:35-44`; sensitivity logic exists only in the separate demo endpoint/service at `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/message/MessageController.java:24-37` and `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/message/MessagePrivacyService.java:9-27`
35:   - Impact: The prompt requires desensitization "based on sensitivity levels," but the actual inbox path does not carry or enforce sensitivity levels.
36:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add sensitivity metadata to queued/inbox messages and apply `MessagePrivacyService` during message delivery/rendering.
37: 3. Client-side route authorization is absent; browser users can navigate directly to admin/dispatcher pages and rely solely on backend rejection.
38:   - Evidence: `frontend/src/app/app.routes.ts:10-18`, `frontend/src/main.ts:9-14`, no route guard definitions found under `frontend/src/app/`
39:   - Impact: This is not a server-side privilege-escalation bug because backend RBAC remains enforced, but it is still a feature-level authorization weakness and a weaker real-product experience.
40:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add route guards for authentication and role-aware navigation hiding.
41: 4. Test coverage is not sufficient to catch the vast majority of core defects.
42:   - Evidence: no frontend specs under `fullstack/frontend/src`; backend tests listed in `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:10-19` and `backend/src/test/java/**`; `AuthControllerTest` exists at `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/AuthControllerTest.java:1-79` but is omitted from the default unit runner in `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:10-19`; API coverage is narrow in `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh:229-278`
43:   - Impact: Major flows can regress while the provided test entry points still appear acceptable.
44:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Expand automated tests for message-center ownership, workflow escalation, import HTML/versioning, admin-template application, search edge cases, and frontend component flows.
45: 
46: ### Low
47: 
48: 1. README startup instructions are present but incomplete because they do not explicitly tell the operator to create `.env` from `.env.example` before Docker Compose startup.
49:   - Evidence: `README.md:9-29`, required environment values in `.env.example:1-17`, backend startup validation in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/config/SecurityStartupValidator.java:37-75`
50:   - Impact: First-time operators can hit backend startup failures or blank compose variables even though the repo already provides the example file.
51:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add `cp .env.example .env` to README before `docker-compose up --build`.
52:

56: 
57: - Conclusion: `Partially Pass`
58: - Reason (Theoretical Basis): Acceptance requires clear startup instructions and verifiable execution without core-code modification. This repo provides a concrete startup path and test/build scripts, but full runtime verification is only partially achievable in the current environment because backend prerequisites are missing here and the README does not fully document the `.env` preparation step.

Full-stack runtime matching is only partially confirmable here because the backend could not be launched in this environment and Docker startup was intentionally not performed.

91: ### 1.3 Does the deliverable severely deviate from the Prompt theme?
92: 
93: - Conclusion: `Partially Pass`
94: - Reason (Theoretical Basis): The codebase does revolve around the requested passenger/dispatcher/admin bus-operations scenario, but the repo is still framed as an "Authentication & Security Foundation" and some core business wiring remains incomplete, so the fit is real but not fully faithful.

104: ## 2. Delivery Completeness
105: 
106: ### 2.1 Does the deliverable completely cover the core requirements explicitly stated in the Prompt?
107: 
108: - Conclusion: `Fail`
109: - Reason (Theoretical Basis): Acceptance clause 2.1 asks for complete coverage of explicit core requirements. This project covers many major features, but several prompt-critical links are incomplete: imported/admin-managed data does not feed search, workflow escalation is manual, runtime templates/dictionaries are not applied, and sensitivity-level masking is not part of the actual inbox pipeline.
110: - Evidence:

113:   - Message center and scheduled queue implemented: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:34-99`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageQueueService.java:34-69`, `frontend/src/app/message-center.component.ts:65-97`

119:     - sensitivity levels not used in inbox pipeline: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/message/MessagePrivacyService.java:9-27`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageEntity.java:23-46`
120: - Reproducible Verification Method:

129: ### 2.2 Does the deliverable have a basic 0-to-1 delivery form rather than fragmentary/demo-only code?
130: 
131: - Conclusion: `Pass`
132: - Reason (Theoretical Basis): The repo contains a coherent full-stack structure, backend/frontend separation, schema, documentation, test scripts, and multi-screen Angular UI. This is materially beyond a single-file demo or fragmentary prototype.
133: - Evidence: repo structure from `README.md:3-7`; full schema in `backend/src/main/resources/schema.sql:1-191`; Angular routes in `frontend/src/app/app.routes.ts:10-18`; Docker composition in `docker-compose.yml:1-49`; docs in `docs/design.md:1-139` and `docs/api-spec.md:1-61`
134: - Reproducible Verification Method:
135:   - `cd /Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91 && rg --files fullstack`
136:   - Expected result: backend, frontend, docs, test scripts, backup scripts, and configuration files all exist as a cohesive project.
137: 
138: ## 3. Engineering and Architecture Quality
139: 
140: ### 3.1 Is the engineering structure and module division reasonable for the problem scale?
141: 
142: - Conclusion: `Pass`
143: - Reason (Theoretical Basis): The project is separated into domain-focused backend packages (`auth`, `search`, `workflow`, `messagecenter`, `admin`, `integration`, `monitoring`) and a frontend split into role-specific Angular screens and services. Responsibilities are mostly clear and not stacked into a single file.
144: - Evidence: backend package layout from `rg --files`; representative controllers/services in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/search/SearchController.java:9-22`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowController.java:20-89`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/admin/AdminControlController.java:21-97`; frontend route/component split in `frontend/src/app/app.routes.ts:10-18`
145: - Reproducible Verification Method:
146:   - `cd /Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack`
147:   - `rg --files backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security frontend/src/app`
148:   - Expected result: modules are grouped by concern instead of being concentrated in one or two files.
149: 
150: ### 3.2 Does the deliverable show maintainability and extensibility awareness?
151: 
152: - Conclusion: `Partially Pass`
153: - Reason (Theoretical Basis): The code shows extensibility patterns such as DB-backed rule weights, configurable cleaning rules, DTO validation, and service/controller separation. However, several runtime paths remain hardcoded where extensibility matters most, especially notification template usage, dictionary usage, and the bootstrap search catalog.

155: - Reproducible Verification Method:

345: ### Security Coverage Audit
346: 
347: - Authentication (login/token/session)

350:   - Boundary: existing login test is not in the default unit runner, and token expiry/disabled-user cases are not covered.
351: - Route Authorization
352:   - Conclusion: `Sufficient`
353:   - Evidence: `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/RbacAccessTest.java:42-61`, enforced rules in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/config/SecurityConfig.java:64-70`
354:   - Boundary: server-side authorization is solid; frontend route hiding is not tested and not implemented.
355: - Object-level Authorization
356:   - Conclusion: `Basic Coverage`
357:   - Evidence: workflow test `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowAuthorizationTest.java:58-115`; server checks in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowService.java:122-139` and `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageCenterService.java:43-50`
358:   - Boundary: message ownership has code-level enforcement but no automated test.
359: - Data Isolation
360:   - Conclusion: `Partially Covered`
361:   - Evidence: message ownership and workflow ownership code above; no tenant model in `backend/src/main/resources/schema.sql:1-163`
362:   - Boundary: per-user isolation exists for some resources, but there is no full regression suite around every user-scoped endpoint.
363: 
364: ### Mock / Stub Assessment
365: 
366: - Mock/stub usage is present in unit tests and is acceptable:
367:   - `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/search/SearchServiceTest.java:22-45` uses mocked repositories/services.
368: - No evidence was found of a mock payment flow or similar forbidden substitution.
369: - Risk note:
370:   - The more important delivery risk is not test mocking itself, but that the runtime search catalog depends on seeded sample data in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/search/TransitStopBootstrapService.java:18-33`.
371: 
372: ### Overall Judgment on Whether the Tests Are Sufficient to Catch the Vast Majority of Problems
373: 
374: - Conclusion: `Fail`
375: - Reason (Judgment Boundary): The current tests do cover some important foundations, especially RBAC basics, workflow ownership, search ranking/dedup happy paths, DND logic, JSON-cleaning happy path, and selected API semantics. However, they do not cover enough of the highest-risk business paths to catch the vast majority of serious defects. Missing areas include message-center ownership, notification preference controller validation, workflow mode semantics and automatic escalation, import HTML/audit/versioning, admin runtime template/dictionary usage, observability alert triggering, frontend behavior, and multiple edge/boundary cases. Because of those gaps, the project could still have severe defects while the provided test suite appears healthy.
376: - Minimal improvement suggestions by priority:
377:   1. Add ownership tests for message-center read/delete and add automatic workflow-escalation tests.
378:   2. Add integration tests for notification preferences, search API, admin template application, and import HTML/audit/versioning.
379:   3. Add observability alert-trigger tests and a small frontend test suite for login, route gating, and core screens.
380:   4. Add boundary tests for empty results, time windows, duplicate actions, and malformed inputs.
381: 
382: ## Final Acceptance Summary
383: 
384: - The project is substantial and clearly more than a demo. It contains a real full-stack structure, Angular UI, Spring Boot backend, PostgreSQL schema, queueing, admin screens, workflow features, and observability.
385: - The acceptance result is not a full pass because several prompt-critical behaviors are only partially realized:
386:   - imported/admin-managed data does not power the passenger search experience
387:   - workflow timeout warnings are manual instead of automatic
388:   - admin-maintained templates/dictionaries do not drive runtime behavior
389:   - sensitivity-level masking is separated from the real inbox pipeline
390:   - test coverage is not broad enough for the main risk areas
391: - Acceptance verdict: `Partially Pass`

<content>1: #!/bin/bash
2: set -euo pipefail
3: 
4: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
5: ROOT_DIR="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
6: BACKEND_DIR="${ROOT_DIR}/backend"
7: ERROR_LOG="${ROOT_DIR}/unit_tests/error.log"
8: > "$ERROR_LOG"
9: 
10: TEST_CLASSES=(
11:   "MessagePrivacyServiceTest"
12:   "SearchServiceTest"
13:   "NotificationRuleServiceTest"
14:   "RbacAccessTest"
15:   "ObservabilityServiceTest"
16:   "WorkflowAuthorizationTest"
17:   "DataImportServiceTest"
18:   "ApiSecuritySemanticsTest"
19: )
20: 
21: TOTAL=${#TEST_CLASSES[@]}
22: PASS=0
23: FAIL=0
24: 
25: TEST_ARG=$(IFS=,; echo "${TEST_CLASSES[*]}")
26: 
27: run_maven_local() {
28:   bash -lc "cd \"${BACKEND_DIR}\" && mvn -q -Dtest=${TEST_ARG} test"
29: }
30: 
31: run_maven_docker() {
32:   local m2_volume="mindflow-m2-cache"
33:   bash -lc "MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1 docker run --rm -v \"${BACKEND_DIR}:/app\" -v \"${m2_volume}:/root/.m2\" -w \"/app\" maven:3.9.8-eclipse-temurin-17-alpine mvn -q -Dtest=${TEST_ARG} test"
34: }
35: 
36: echo "[UNIT] Running ${TOTAL} tests in one Maven invocation"
37: echo "[UNIT] Targets: ${TEST_ARG}"
38: 
39: if command -v mvn >/dev/null 2>&1; then
40:   if run_maven_local; then
41:     PASS=$TOTAL
42:   else
43:     FAIL=$TOTAL
44:     echo "[UNIT][FAIL] Maven local run failed" >> "$ERROR_LOG"
45:   fi
46: elif command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
47:   if run_maven_docker; then
48:     PASS=$TOTAL
49:   else
50:     FAIL=$TOTAL
51:     echo "[UNIT][FAIL] Maven docker run failed" >> "$ERROR_LOG"
52:   fi
53: else
54:   FAIL=$TOTAL
55:   echo "[UNIT] neither mvn nor docker found in environment" | tee -a "$ERROR_LOG"
56: fi
57: 
58: echo "UNIT_SUMMARY total=${TOTAL} pass=${PASS} fail=${FAIL}"
59: if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
60:   echo "Failure logs: ${ERROR_LOG}"
61:   exit 1
62: fi

<path>C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\mindFlow\TASK-20260318-393C91\fullstack\backend\src\main\java\com\mindflow\security\workflow\WorkflowService.java</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.workflow;
2: 
3: import com.mindflow.security.common.OwnershipDeniedException;
4: import com.mindflow.security.common.ResourceNotFoundException;
5: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
6: import org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;
7: 
8: import java.time.Duration;
9: import java.time.Instant;
10: import java.util.LinkedHashMap;
11: import java.util.List;
12: import java.util.Map;
13: import java.util.Set;
14: 
15: @Service
16: public class WorkflowService {
17: 
18:     private static final Duration ESCALATION_TIMEOUT = Duration.ofHours(24);
19: 
20:     private final WorkflowTaskRepository repository;
21:     private final WorkflowStateMachineService stateMachineService;
22: 
23:     public WorkflowService(WorkflowTaskRepository repository,
24:                            WorkflowStateMachineService stateMachineService) {
25:         this.repository = repository;
26:         this.stateMachineService = stateMachineService;
27:     }
28: 
29:     @Transactional
30:     public WorkflowTaskResponse createTask(CreateWorkflowTaskRequest request, String submittedBy) {
31:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = new WorkflowTaskEntity();
32:         task.setType(request.type());
33:         task.setMode(request.mode());
34:         task.setStatus(WorkflowStatus.SUBMITTED);
35:         task.setTitle(request.title());
36:         task.setPayload(request.payload());
37:         task.setSubmittedBy(submittedBy);
38:         task.setAssignedTo(submittedBy);
39:         Set<String> collaborators = task.collaboratorsAsSet();
40:         collaborators.add(submittedBy);
41:         if (request.collaborators() != null) {
42:             collaborators.addAll(request.collaborators());
43:         }
44:         task.setCollaboratorsFromSet(collaborators);
45:         task.setCurrentStep(1);
46:         task.setTotalSteps(3);
47:         int required = request.requiredApprovals() == null ? defaultApprovals(request.mode()) : Math.max(1, request.requiredApprovals());
48:         task.setRequiredApprovals(required);
49:         task.setReceivedApprovals(0);
50:         task.setEscalated(false);
51:         task.setLastActionAt(Instant.now());
52:         return toResponse(repository.save(task));
53:     }
54: 
55:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
56:     public List<WorkflowTaskResponse> listTasks(String actor) {
57:         Map<Long, WorkflowTaskResponse> merged = new LinkedHashMap<>();
58:         for (WorkflowTaskEntity task : repository.findByAssignedToOrderByCreatedAtDesc(actor)) {
59:             merged.put(task.getId(), toResponse(task));
60:         }
61:         for (WorkflowTaskEntity task : repository.findByCollaboratorsContainingOrderByCreatedAtDesc(actor)) {
62:             if (canAccess(task, actor)) {
63:                 merged.put(task.getId(), toResponse(task));
64:             }
65:         }
66:         return merged.values().stream().toList();
67:     }
68: 
69:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
70:     public WorkflowDetailsResponse getTask(Long id, String actor) {
71:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = fetch(id, actor);
72:         return new WorkflowDetailsResponse(toResponse(task), buildProgress(task));
73:     }
74: 
75:     @Transactional
76:     public WorkflowTaskResponse approve(Long id, String actor) {
77:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = fetch(id, actor);
78:         stateMachineService.approve(task);
79:         task.setLastActionAt(Instant.now());
80:         task.setEscalated(false);
81:         return toResponse(repository.save(task));
82:     }
83: 
84:     @Transactional
85:     public WorkflowTaskResponse reject(Long id, String actor) {
86:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = fetch(id, actor);
87:         stateMachineService.reject(task);
88:         task.setLastActionAt(Instant.now());
89:         task.setEscalated(false);
90:         return toResponse(repository.save(task));
91:     }
92: 
93:     @Transactional
94:     public WorkflowTaskResponse returnToSubmitter(Long id, String actor) {
95:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = fetch(id, actor);
96:         stateMachineService.returnToSubmitter(task);
97:         task.setLastActionAt(Instant.now());
98:         task.setEscalated(false);
99:         return toResponse(repository.save(task));
100:     }
101: 
102:     @Transactional
103:     public List<WorkflowTaskResponse> batchApprove(List<Long> ids, String actor) {
104:         return ids.stream().map(id -> approve(id, actor)).toList();
105:     }
106: 
107:     @Transactional
108:     public List<WorkflowTaskResponse> evaluateEscalations() {
109:         Instant cutoff = Instant.now().minus(ESCALATION_TIMEOUT);
110:         List<WorkflowTaskEntity> staleTasks = repository.findByStatusInAndLastActionAtBefore(
111:                 List.of(WorkflowStatus.SUBMITTED, WorkflowStatus.IN_REVIEW),
112:                 cutoff
113:         );
114: 
115:         for (WorkflowTaskEntity task : staleTasks) {
116:             task.setEscalated(true);
117:         }
118:         repository.saveAll(staleTasks);
119:         return staleTasks.stream().map(this::toResponse).toList();
120:     }
121: 
122:     private WorkflowTaskEntity fetch(Long id, String actor) {
123:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = repository.findById(id)
124:                 .orElseThrow(() -> new ResourceNotFoundException("Task not found"));
125:         if (!canAccess(task, actor)) {
126:             throw new OwnershipDeniedException("Workflow task access denied for this user");
127:         }
128:         return task;
129:     }
130: 
131:     private boolean canAccess(WorkflowTaskEntity task, String actor) {
132:         if (actor.equals(task.getAssignedTo())) {
133:             return true;
134:         }
135:         if (task.getMode() == WorkflowMode.CONDITIONAL) {
136:             return false;
137:         }
138:         return task.collaboratorsAsSet().contains(actor);
139:     }
140: 
141:     private int defaultApprovals(WorkflowMode mode) {
142:         return switch (mode) {
143:             case JOINT, PARALLEL -> 2;
144:             case CONDITIONAL -> 1;
145:         };
146:     }
147: 
148:     private List<WorkflowProgressStep> buildProgress(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
149:         return List.of(
150:                 new WorkflowProgressStep("Submitted", task.getCurrentStep() >= 1, task.getCurrentStep() == 1),
151:                 new WorkflowProgressStep("In Review", task.getCurrentStep() >= 2, task.getCurrentStep() == 2),
152:                 new WorkflowProgressStep("Final Decision", task.getCurrentStep() >= 3 || isFinal(task), task.getCurrentStep() == 3 || isFinal(task))
153:         );
154:     }
155: 
156:     private boolean isFinal(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
157:         return task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.APPROVED ||
158:                 task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.REJECTED ||
159:                 task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.RETURNED;
160:     }
161: 
162:     private WorkflowTaskResponse toResponse(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
163:         boolean timeout = Duration.between(task.getLastActionAt(), Instant.now()).toHours() >= 24
164:                 && (task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.SUBMITTED || task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.IN_REVIEW);
165:         return new WorkflowTaskResponse(
166:                 task.getId(),
167:                 task.getType(),
168:                 task.getMode(),
169:                 task.getStatus(),
170:                 task.getTitle(),
171:                 task.getPayload(),
172:                 task.getSubmittedBy(),
173:                 task.getAssignedTo(),
174:                 task.getCurrentStep(),
175:                 task.getTotalSteps(),
176:                 task.getRequiredApprovals(),
177:                 task.getReceivedApprovals(),
178:                 task.isEscalated(),
179:                 timeout,
180:                 task.getLastActionAt(),
181:                 task.getCreatedAt()
182:         );
183:     }
184: }

(End of file - total 184 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.messagecenter;
2: 
3: import com.mindflow.security.common.OwnershipDeniedException;
4: import com.mindflow.security.common.ResourceNotFoundException;
5: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
6: import org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;
7: 
8: import java.util.List;
9: 
10: @Service
11: public class MessageCenterService {
12: 
13:     private final MessageRepository messageRepository;
14:     private final MessageMaskingService maskingService;
15: 
16:     public MessageCenterService(MessageRepository messageRepository,
17:                                 MessageMaskingService maskingService) {
18:         this.messageRepository = messageRepository;
19:         this.maskingService = maskingService;
20:     }
21: 
22:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
23:     public List<MessageResponse> listMessages(String username, MessageType type) {
24:         List<MessageEntity> rows = type == null
25:                 ? messageRepository.findByUsernameOrderByCreatedAtDesc(username)
26:                 : messageRepository.findByUsernameAndTypeOrderByCreatedAtDesc(username, type);
27:         return rows.stream().map(this::toResponse).toList();
28:     }
29: 
30:     @Transactional
31:     public MessageResponse markRead(String username, Long id, boolean read) {
32:         MessageEntity row = findOwned(username, id);
33:         row.setRead(read);
34:         return toResponse(messageRepository.save(row));
35:     }
36: 
37:     @Transactional
38:     public void delete(String username, Long id) {
39:         MessageEntity row = findOwned(username, id);
40:         messageRepository.delete(row);
41:     }
42: 
43:     private MessageEntity findOwned(String username, Long id) {
44:         MessageEntity row = messageRepository.findById(id)
45:                 .orElseThrow(() -> new ResourceNotFoundException("Message not found"));
46:         if (!row.getUsername().equals(username)) {
47:             throw new OwnershipDeniedException("Message access denied for this user");
48:         }
49:         return row;
50:     }
51: 
52:     private MessageResponse toResponse(MessageEntity row) {
53:         String maskedContent = maskingService.mask(row.getContent());
54:         return new MessageResponse(
55:                 row.getId(),
56:                 row.getType(),
57:                 row.getTitle(),
58:                 maskedContent,
59:                 row.isRead(),
60:                 !maskedContent.equals(row.getContent()),
61:                 row.getCreatedAt()
62:         );
63:     }
64: }

(End of file - total 64 lines)
</content>

<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.messagecenter;
2: 
3: import jakarta.validation.Valid;
4: import jakarta.validation.constraints.Positive;
5: import org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;
6: import org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;
7: import org.springframework.security.access.prepost.PreAuthorize;
8: import org.springframework.security.core.Authentication;
9: import org.springframework.validation.annotation.Validated;
10: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.DeleteMapping;
11: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;
12: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PathVariable;
13: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PostMapping;
14: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PutMapping;
15: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestBody;
16: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;
17: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestParam;
18: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;
19: 
20: import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
21: import java.security.MessageDigest;
22: import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;
23: import java.util.List;
24: import java.util.Map;
25: 
26: @RestController
27: @RequestMapping("/api/passenger/messages-center")
28: @Validated
29: public class MessageCenterController {
30: 
31:     private final MessageCenterService messageCenterService;
32:     private final BookingEventRepository bookingRepository;
33: 
34:     public MessageCenterController(MessageCenterService messageCenterService,
35:                                    BookingEventRepository bookingRepository) {
36:         this.messageCenterService = messageCenterService;
37:         this.bookingRepository = bookingRepository;
38:     }
39: 
40:     @GetMapping
41:     @PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole('PASSENGER', 'DISPATCHER', 'ADMIN')")
42:     public ResponseEntity<List<MessageResponse>> list(
43:             @RequestParam(name = "type", required = false) MessageType type,
44:             Authentication authentication) {
45:         return ResponseEntity.ok(messageCenterService.listMessages(authentication.getName(), type));
46:     }
47: 
48:     @PutMapping("/{id}/read")
49:     @PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole('PASSENGER', 'DISPATCHER', 'ADMIN')")
50:     public ResponseEntity<MessageResponse> markRead(
51:             @PathVariable @Positive Long id,
52:             @Valid @RequestBody MessageReadRequest request,
53:             Authentication authentication) {
54:         return ResponseEntity.ok(messageCenterService.markRead(authentication.getName(), id, request.read()));
55:     }
56: 
57:     @DeleteMapping("/{id}")
58:     @PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole('PASSENGER', 'DISPATCHER', 'ADMIN')")
59:     public ResponseEntity<Void> delete(
60:             @PathVariable @Positive Long id,
61:             Authentication authentication) {
62:         messageCenterService.delete(authentication.getName(), id);
63:         return ResponseEntity.noContent().build();
64:     }
65: 
66:     @PostMapping("/booking-events")
67:     @PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole('PASSENGER', 'DISPATCHER', 'ADMIN')")
68:     public ResponseEntity<Map<String, Long>> createBooking(
69:             @Valid @RequestBody BookingEventRequest request,
70:             Authentication authentication) {
71:         BookingEventEntity event = new BookingEventEntity();
72:         event.setUsername(authentication.getName());
73:         event.setRouteNumber(request.routeNumber());
74:         event.setPassengerPhoneToken(hashToken(request.passengerPhone()));
75:         event.setPassengerIdCardToken(hashToken(request.passengerIdCard()));
76:         event.setStartTime(request.startTime());
77:         event.setReservationSuccessSent(false);
78:         event.setArrivalReminderSent(false);
79:         event.setMissedCheckInSent(false);
80:         BookingEventEntity saved = bookingRepository.save(event);
81:         return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.CREATED).body(Map.of("bookingEventId", saved.getId()));
82:     }
83: 
84:     private String hashToken(String value) {
85:         try {
86:             MessageDigest digest = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256");
87:             byte[] out = digest.digest(value.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8));
88:             StringBuilder hex = new StringBuilder();
89:             for (byte b : out) {
90:                 hex.append(String.format("%02x", b));
91:             }
92:             return hex.toString();
93:         } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException ex) {
94:             throw new IllegalStateException("SHA-256 unavailable", ex);
95:         }
96:     }
97: }

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.workflow;
2: 
3: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
4: 
5: @Service
6: public class WorkflowStateMachineService {
7: 
8:     public WorkflowTaskEntity approve(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
9:         ensureActionable(task);
10:         switch (task.getMode()) {
11:             case JOINT -> approveJoint(task);
12:             case PARALLEL -> approveParallel(task);
13:             case CONDITIONAL -> approveConditional(task);
14:         }
15:         return task;
16:     }
17: 
18:     public WorkflowTaskEntity reject(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
19:         ensureActionable(task);
20:         task.setStatus(WorkflowStatus.REJECTED);
21:         return task;
22:     }
23: 
24:     public WorkflowTaskEntity returnToSubmitter(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
25:         ensureActionable(task);
26:         task.setStatus(WorkflowStatus.RETURNED);
27:         task.setCurrentStep(1);
28:         task.setReceivedApprovals(0);
29:         return task;
30:     }
31: 
32:     private void ensureActionable(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
33:         if (task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.APPROVED ||
34:                 task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.REJECTED ||
35:                 task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.RETURNED) {
36:             throw new IllegalArgumentException("Task is already finalized");
37:         }
38:     }
39: 
40:     private void approveJoint(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
41:         task.setReceivedApprovals(task.getReceivedApprovals() + 1);
42:         task.setStatus(WorkflowStatus.IN_REVIEW);
43:         task.setCurrentStep(2);
44:         if (task.getReceivedApprovals() >= task.getRequiredApprovals()) {
45:             task.setStatus(WorkflowStatus.APPROVED);
46:             task.setCurrentStep(task.getTotalSteps());
47:         }
48:     }
49: 
50:     private void approveParallel(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
51:         task.setReceivedApprovals(task.getReceivedApprovals() + 1);
52:         if (task.getReceivedApprovals() >= task.getRequiredApprovals()) {
53:             task.setStatus(WorkflowStatus.APPROVED);
54:             task.setCurrentStep(task.getTotalSteps());
55:         } else {
56:             task.setStatus(WorkflowStatus.IN_REVIEW);
57:             task.setCurrentStep(Math.min(task.getTotalSteps(), task.getCurrentStep() + 1));
58:         }
59:     }
60: 
61:     private void approveConditional(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
62:         task.setStatus(WorkflowStatus.IN_REVIEW);
63:         if (task.getCurrentStep() == 1 && task.getPayload() != null && task.getPayload().toLowerCase().contains("urgent")) {
64:             task.setCurrentStep(Math.min(task.getTotalSteps(), task.getCurrentStep() + 2));
65:         } else {
66:             task.setCurrentStep(Math.min(task.getTotalSteps(), task.getCurrentStep() + 1));
67:         }
68:         if (task.getCurrentStep() >= task.getTotalSteps()) {
69:             task.setStatus(WorkflowStatus.APPROVED);
70:         }
71:     }
72: }

<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.common;
2: 
3: import com.mindflow.security.monitoring.TraceContext;
4: import jakarta.validation.ConstraintViolationException;
5: import org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;
6: import org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;
7: import org.springframework.http.converter.HttpMessageNotReadableException;
8: import org.springframework.security.access.AccessDeniedException;
9: import org.springframework.security.authentication.BadCredentialsException;
10: import org.springframework.validation.FieldError;
11: import org.springframework.web.bind.MethodArgumentNotValidException;
12: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.ExceptionHandler;
13: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestControllerAdvice;
14: import org.springframework.web.method.annotation.MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException;
15: 
16: import java.time.Instant;
17: import java.util.stream.Collectors;
18: 
19: @RestControllerAdvice
20: public class GlobalExceptionHandler {
21: 
22:     @ExceptionHandler(MethodArgumentNotValidException.class)
23:     public ResponseEntity<ApiErrorResponse> handleValidation(MethodArgumentNotValidException ex) {
24:         String message = ex.getBindingResult().getFieldErrors().stream()
25:                 .map(this::formatFieldError)
26:                 .collect(Collectors.joining("; "));
27:         return error(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, message.isBlank() ? "Validation failed" : message);
28:     }
29: 
30:     @ExceptionHandler(ConstraintViolationException.class)
31:     public ResponseEntity<ApiErrorResponse> handleConstraintViolation(ConstraintViolationException ex) {
32:         return error(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, ex.getMessage());
33:     }
34: 
35:     @ExceptionHandler({HttpMessageNotReadableException.class, MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException.class})
36:     public ResponseEntity<ApiErrorResponse> handleMalformed(Exception ex) {
37:         return error(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "Malformed request payload or parameter");
38:     }
39: 
40:     @ExceptionHandler(BadCredentialsException.class)
41:     public ResponseEntity<ApiErrorResponse> handleCredentials() {
42:         return error(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED, "Invalid username or password");
43:     }
44: 
45:     @ExceptionHandler(AccessDeniedException.class)
46:     public ResponseEntity<ApiErrorResponse> handleAccessDenied() {
47:         return error(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, "Access denied");
48:     }
49: 
50:     @ExceptionHandler(IllegalArgumentException.class)
51:     public ResponseEntity<ApiErrorResponse> handleIllegalArgument(IllegalArgumentException ex) {
52:         return error(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, ex.getMessage());
53:     }
54: 
55:     @ExceptionHandler(ResourceNotFoundException.class)
56:     public ResponseEntity<ApiErrorResponse> handleNotFound(ResourceNotFoundException ex) {
57:         return error(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND, ex.getMessage());
58:     }
59: 
60:     @ExceptionHandler(ResourceConflictException.class)
61:     public ResponseEntity<ApiErrorResponse> handleConflict(ResourceConflictException ex) {
62:         return error(HttpStatus.CONFLICT, ex.getMessage());
63:     }
64: 
65:     @ExceptionHandler(OwnershipDeniedException.class)
66:     public ResponseEntity<ApiErrorResponse> handleOwnershipDenied(OwnershipDeniedException ex) {
67:         return error(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, ex.getMessage());
68:     }
69: 
70:     @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class)
71:     public ResponseEntity<ApiErrorResponse> handleUnexpected(Exception ex) {
72:         return error(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, "Internal server error");
73:     }
74: 
75:     private String formatFieldError(FieldError error) {
76:         return error.getField() + " " + (error.getDefaultMessage() == null ? "is invalid" : error.getDefaultMessage());
77:     }
78: 
79:     private ResponseEntity<ApiErrorResponse> error(HttpStatus status, String msg) {
80:         return ResponseEntity.status(status).body(new ApiErrorResponse(
81:                 status.value(),
82:                 msg,
83:                 TraceContext.getTraceId(),
84:                 Instant.now()
85:         ));
86:     }
87: }

<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.auth;
2: 
3: import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationManager;
4: import org.springframework.security.authentication.UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken;
5: import org.springframework.security.core.Authentication;
6: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
7: 
8: @Service
9: public class AuthService {
10: 
11:     private final AuthenticationManager authenticationManager;
12:     private final JwtService jwtService;
13: 
14:     public AuthService(AuthenticationManager authenticationManager, JwtService jwtService) {
15:         this.authenticationManager = authenticationManager;
16:         this.jwtService = jwtService;
17:     }
18: 
19:     public LoginResponse login(LoginRequest request) {
20:         Authentication authentication = authenticationManager.authenticate(
21:                 new UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken(request.username(), request.password())
22:         );
23:         UserPrincipal principal = (UserPrincipal) authentication.getPrincipal();
24:         String token = jwtService.issueToken(principal.getUsername(), principal.getRole());
25:         return new LoginResponse(token, "Bearer", jwtService.getExpirationSeconds(), principal.getUsername(), principal.getRole());

<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.notification;
2: 
3: import com.mindflow.security.common.ResourceNotFoundException;
4: import com.mindflow.security.user.UserEntity;
5: import com.mindflow.security.user.UserRepository;
6: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
7: import org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;
8: 
9: import java.time.LocalTime;
10: 
11: @Service
12: public class NotificationPreferenceService {
13: 
14:     private final UserRepository userRepository;
15:     private final NotificationRuleService notificationRuleService;
16: 
17:     public NotificationPreferenceService(UserRepository userRepository,
18:                                          NotificationRuleService notificationRuleService) {
19:         this.userRepository = userRepository;
20:         this.notificationRuleService = notificationRuleService;
21:     }
22: 
23:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
24:     public NotificationPreferencesResponse getPreferences(String username) {
25:         UserEntity user = findUser(username);
26:         return toResponse(user);
27:     }
28: 
29:     @Transactional
30:     public NotificationPreferencesResponse updatePreferences(String username, NotificationPreferencesRequest request) {
31:         UserEntity user = findUser(username);
32: 
33:         user.setArrivalReminderEnabled(request.arrivalReminderEnabled());
34:         user.setReservationSuccessEnabled(request.reservationSuccessEnabled());
35:         user.setReminderLeadMinutes(request.reminderLeadMinutes());
36: 
37:         boolean dndEnabled = Boolean.TRUE.equals(request.dndEnabled());
38:         if (dndEnabled) {
39:             if (request.dndStart() == null || request.dndEnd() == null) {
40:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException("DND start and end are required when DND is enabled");
41:             }
42:             user.setDndStart(LocalTime.parse(request.dndStart()));
43:             user.setDndEnd(LocalTime.parse(request.dndEnd()));
44:         } else {
45:             user.setDndStart(null);
46:             user.setDndEnd(null);
47:         }
48: 
49:         UserEntity saved = userRepository.save(user);
50:         return toResponse(saved);
51:     }
52: 
53:     private NotificationPreferencesResponse toResponse(UserEntity user) {
54:         return new NotificationPreferencesResponse(
55:                 user.getUsername(),
56:                 user.isArrivalReminderEnabled(),
57:                 user.isReservationSuccessEnabled(),
58:                 user.getReminderLeadMinutes(),
59:                 user.getDndStart() != null && user.getDndEnd() != null,
60:                 user.getDndStart() == null ? null : user.getDndStart().toString(),
61:                 user.getDndEnd() == null ? null : user.getDndEnd().toString(),
62:                 notificationRuleService.isWithinDndWindow(user, LocalTime.now())
63:         );
64:     }
65: 
66:     private UserEntity findUser(String username) {
67:         return userRepository.findByUsername(username)
68:                 .orElseThrow(() -> new ResourceNotFoundException("User not found"));
69:     }
70: }

(End of file - total 70 lines)
</content>

<path>C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\mindFlow\TASK-20260318-393C91\fullstack\backend\src\main\java\com\mindflow\security\search\SearchService.java</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.search;
2: 
3: import com.mindflow.security.admin.SystemConfigRepository;
4: import com.mindflow.security.monitoring.ObservabilityService;
5: import io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry;
6: import io.micrometer.core.instrument.Timer;
7: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
8: 
9: import java.util.ArrayList;
10: import java.util.Arrays;
11: import java.util.Comparator;
12: import java.util.LinkedHashMap;
13: import java.util.List;
14: import java.util.Locale;
15: import java.util.Map;
16: import java.util.stream.Collectors;
17: 
18: @Service
19: public class SearchService {
20: 
21:     private static final int MAX_RESULTS = 20;
22:     private static final int MAX_SUGGESTIONS = 8;
23:     private static final String KEY_RELEVANCE = "search.weight.relevance";
24:     private static final String KEY_FREQUENCY = "search.weight.frequency";
25:     private static final String KEY_POPULARITY = "search.weight.popularity";
26: 
27:     private final SystemConfigRepository systemConfigRepository;
28:     private final TransitStopRepository transitStopRepository;
29:     private final MeterRegistry meterRegistry;
30:     private final ObservabilityService observabilityService;
31: 
32:     public SearchService(SystemConfigRepository systemConfigRepository,
33:                          TransitStopRepository transitStopRepository,
34:                          MeterRegistry meterRegistry,
35:                          ObservabilityService observabilityService) {
36:         this.systemConfigRepository = systemConfigRepository;
37:         this.transitStopRepository = transitStopRepository;
38:         this.meterRegistry = meterRegistry;
39:         this.observabilityService = observabilityService;
40:     }
41: 
42:     public SearchResponse search(String query) {
43:         Timer.Sample sample = Timer.start(meterRegistry);
44:         String normalizedQuery = normalize(query);
45:         List<ScoredMatch> matches = new ArrayList<>();
46: 
47:         for (TransitStop stop : loadCatalog()) {
48:             ScoredMatch match = score(stop, normalizedQuery);
49:             if (match != null) {
50:                 matches.add(match);
51:             }
52:         }
53: 
54:         List<ScoredMatch> deduplicated = deduplicateByStop(matches);
55:         deduplicated.sort(scoreComparator());
56: 
57:         List<SearchResult> results = deduplicated.stream()
58:                 .limit(MAX_RESULTS)
59:                 .map(candidate -> new SearchResult(
60:                         candidate.stop().routeNumber(),
61:                         candidate.stop().stopName(),
62:                         candidate.stop().frequencyPriority(),
63:                         candidate.stop().stopPopularity(),
64:                         candidate.rankingScore(),
65:                         candidate.matchType()))
66:                 .toList();
67: 
68:         List<String> suggestions = deduplicated.stream()
69:                 .limit(MAX_SUGGESTIONS)
70:                 .map(candidate -> candidate.stop().routeNumber() + " - " + candidate.stop().stopName())
71:                 .distinct()
72:                 .toList();
73: 
74:         SearchResponse response = new SearchResponse(query == null ? "" : query, suggestions, results);
75:         sample.stop(Timer.builder("app.workflow.search.duration")
76:                 .publishPercentiles(0.5, 0.95, 0.99)
77:                 .register(meterRegistry));
78:         observabilityService.recordWorkflowLog("search", "query='" + response.query() + "' results=" + response.results().size());
79:         return response;
80:     }
81: 
82:     private List<ScoredMatch> deduplicateByStop(List<ScoredMatch> matches) {
83:         Map<String, ScoredMatch> byStop = new LinkedHashMap<>();
84:         for (ScoredMatch match : matches) {
85:             String key = normalize(match.stop().stopName());
86:             byStop.merge(key, match, (existing, incoming) ->
87:                     scoreComparator().compare(existing, incoming) <= 0 ? existing : incoming
88:             );
89:         }
90:         return new ArrayList<>(byStop.values());
91:     }
92: 
93:     private Comparator<ScoredMatch> scoreComparator() {
94:         return Comparator
95:                 .comparingLong(ScoredMatch::rankingScore)
96:                 .reversed()
97:                 .thenComparing(match -> match.stop().stopName());
98:     }
99: 
100:     private ScoredMatch score(TransitStop stop, String query) {
101:         if (query.isBlank()) {
102:             long rankingScore = scoreFormula(0, stop.frequencyPriority(), stop.stopPopularity());
103:             return new ScoredMatch(stop, rankingScore, "popular");
104:         }
105: 
106:         String route = normalize(stop.routeNumber());
107:         String stopName = normalize(stop.stopName());
108:         String pinyin = normalize(stop.pinyin());
109:         String initials = normalize(stop.initials());
110:         String keywordCombined = stop.keywords().stream().map(this::normalize).collect(Collectors.joining(" "));
111: 
112:         int relevance = 0;
113:         String matchType = "keyword";
114: 
115:         if (route.equals(query)) {
116:             relevance = 6;
117:             matchType = "route-exact";
118:         } else if (route.startsWith(query)) {
119:             relevance = 5;
120:             matchType = "route-prefix";
121:         } else if (stopName.startsWith(query)) {
122:             relevance = 5;
123:             matchType = "stop-prefix";
124:         } else if (pinyin.startsWith(query) || initials.startsWith(query)) {
125:             relevance = 5;
126:             matchType = "pinyin-prefix";
127:         } else if (keywordCombined.contains(query)) {
128:             relevance = 4;
129:             matchType = "keyword";
130:         } else if (stopName.contains(query)) {
131:             relevance = 3;
132:             matchType = "stop-contains";
133:         } else if (pinyin.contains(query) || initials.contains(query)) {
134:             relevance = 3;
135:             matchType = "pinyin-contains";
136:         }
137: 
138:         if (relevance == 0) {
139:             return null;
140:         }
141: 
142:         long rankingScore = scoreFormula(relevance, stop.frequencyPriority(), stop.stopPopularity());
143:         return new ScoredMatch(stop, rankingScore, matchType);
144:     }
145: 
146:     private long scoreFormula(int relevance, int frequencyPriority, int stopPopularity) {
147:         int relevanceWeight = getWeight(KEY_RELEVANCE, 1_000_000);
148:         int frequencyWeight = getWeight(KEY_FREQUENCY, 1_000);
149:         int popularityWeight = getWeight(KEY_POPULARITY, 1);
150:         return (long) relevance * relevanceWeight
151:                 + (long) frequencyPriority * frequencyWeight
152:                 + (long) stopPopularity * popularityWeight;
153:     }
154: 
155:     private int getWeight(String key, int fallback) {
156:         return systemConfigRepository.findByConfigKey(key)
157:                 .map(config -> {
158:                     try {
159:                         return Integer.parseInt(config.getConfigValue());
160:                     } catch (NumberFormatException ignored) {
161:                         return fallback;
162:                     }
163:                 })
164:                 .orElse(fallback);
165:     }
166: 
167:     private List<TransitStop> loadCatalog() {
168:         return transitStopRepository.findAll().stream()
169:                 .map(this::mapEntity)
170:                 .toList();
171:     }
172: 
173:     private TransitStop mapEntity(TransitStopEntity row) {
174:         List<String> keywords = Arrays.stream(row.getKeywords().split(","))
175:                 .map(String::trim)
176:                 .filter(part -> !part.isBlank())
177:                 .toList();
178:         return new TransitStop(
179:                 row.getRouteNumber(),
180:                 row.getStopName(),
181:                 keywords,
182:                 row.getPinyin(),
183:                 row.getInitials(),
184:                 row.getFrequencyPriority(),
185:                 row.getStopPopularity()
186:         );
187:     }
188: 
189:     private String normalize(String value) {
190:         return value == null ? "" : value.trim().toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);
191:     }

<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.integration;
2: 
3: import com.fasterxml.jackson.core.type.TypeReference;
4: import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper;
5: import com.mindflow.security.admin.AdminControlService;
6: import com.mindflow.security.monitoring.ObservabilityService;
7: import io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry;
8: import io.micrometer.core.instrument.Timer;
9: import org.jsoup.Jsoup;
10: import org.jsoup.nodes.Document;
11: import org.jsoup.nodes.Element;
12: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
13: import org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;
14: 
15: import java.util.ArrayList;
16: import java.util.HashMap;
17: import java.util.List;
18: import java.util.Locale;
19: import java.util.Map;
20: import java.util.Optional;
21: import java.util.regex.Matcher;
22: import java.util.regex.Pattern;
23: 
24: @Service
25: public class DataImportService {
26: 
27:     private static final Pattern FIRST_NUMBER = Pattern.compile("\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)?");
28: 
29:     private final ImportJobRepository importJobRepository;
30:     private final CleanedRecordRepository cleanedRecordRepository;
31:     private final CleaningAuditRepository cleaningAuditRepository;
32:     private final StopStructureVersionRepository stopStructureVersionRepository;
33:     private final AdminControlService adminControlService;
34:     private final ObjectMapper objectMapper;
35:     private final MeterRegistry meterRegistry;
36:     private final ObservabilityService observabilityService;
37: 
38:     public DataImportService(ImportJobRepository importJobRepository,
39:                              CleanedRecordRepository cleanedRecordRepository,
40:                              CleaningAuditRepository cleaningAuditRepository,
41:                              StopStructureVersionRepository stopStructureVersionRepository,
42:                              AdminControlService adminControlService,
43:                              ObjectMapper objectMapper,
44:                              MeterRegistry meterRegistry,
45:                              ObservabilityService observabilityService) {
46:         this.importJobRepository = importJobRepository;
47:         this.cleanedRecordRepository = cleanedRecordRepository;
48:         this.cleaningAuditRepository = cleaningAuditRepository;
49:         this.stopStructureVersionRepository = stopStructureVersionRepository;
50:         this.adminControlService = adminControlService;
51:         this.objectMapper = objectMapper;
52:         this.meterRegistry = meterRegistry;
53:         this.observabilityService = observabilityService;
54:     }
55: 
56:     @Transactional
57:     public ImportSummaryResponse ingest(ImportRequest request) {
58:         Timer.Sample sample = Timer.start(meterRegistry);
59:         ImportJobEntity job = new ImportJobEntity();
60:         job.setFormat(request.format());
61:         job.setSourceName(request.sourceName());
62:         job.setStatus(ImportStatus.RUNNING);
63:         job.setTotalRows(0);
64:         job.setSuccessRows(0);
65:         job.setFailedRows(0);
66:         job = importJobRepository.save(job);
67: 
68:         List<String> notes = new ArrayList<>();
69:         try {
70:             List<RawImportRecord> parsed = parse(request.format(), request.payload());
71:             job.setTotalRows(parsed.size());
72: 
73:             for (RawImportRecord raw : parsed) {
74:                 try {
75:                     cleanAndPersist(job.getId(), raw, notes);
76:                     job.setSuccessRows(job.getSuccessRows() + 1);
77:                 } catch (Exception rowEx) {
78:                     job.setFailedRows(job.getFailedRows() + 1);
79:                     notes.add("Failed row " + raw.sourceRef() + ": " + rowEx.getMessage());
80:                 }
81:             }
82: 
83:             job.setStatus(ImportStatus.COMPLETED);
84:         } catch (Exception ex) {
85:             job.setStatus(ImportStatus.FAILED);
86:             notes.add("Import failed: " + ex.getMessage());
87:         }
88:         job = importJobRepository.save(job);
89: 
90:         sample.stop(Timer.builder("app.workflow.parsing.duration")
91:                 .publishPercentiles(0.5, 0.95, 0.99)
92:                 .register(meterRegistry));
93:         observabilityService.recordWorkflowLog("parsing", "source=" + request.sourceName() + " status=" + job.getStatus());
94:         return new ImportSummaryResponse(toJobResponse(job), notes);
95:     }
96: 
97:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
98:     public List<ImportJobResponse> listJobs() {
99:         return importJobRepository.findAll().stream()
100:                 .map(this::toJobResponse)
101:                 .toList();
102:     }
103: 
104:     private List<RawImportRecord> parse(ImportFormat format, String payload) throws Exception {
105:         return switch (format) {
106:             case JSON -> parseJson(payload);
107:             case HTML -> parseHtml(payload);
108:         };
109:     }
110: 
111:     private List<RawImportRecord> parseJson(String payload) throws Exception {
112:         List<Map<String, Object>> rows = objectMapper.readValue(payload, new TypeReference<>() {
113:         });
114:         List<RawImportRecord> parsed = new ArrayList<>();
115:         int i = 1;
116:         for (Map<String, Object> row : rows) {
117:             Map<String, String> fields = new HashMap<>();
118:             row.forEach((k, v) -> fields.put(k, v == null ? null : String.valueOf(v)));
119:             parsed.add(new RawImportRecord("json-row-" + i, fields, objectMapper.writeValueAsString(row)));
120:             i++;
121:         }
122:         return parsed;
123:     }
124: 
125:     private List<RawImportRecord> parseHtml(String payload) {
126:         Document doc = Jsoup.parse(payload);
127:         List<RawImportRecord> parsed = new ArrayList<>();
128:         int i = 1;
129:         for (Element row : doc.select("table tr")) {
130:             if (row.select("th").size() > 0) {
131:                 continue;
132:             }
133:             List<Element> cols = row.select("td");
134:             if (cols.size() < 5) {
135:                 continue;
136:             }
137:             Map<String, String> fields = Map.of(
138:                     "stop", cols.get(0).text(),
139:                     "address", cols.get(1).text(),
140:                     "apartment", cols.get(2).text(),
141:                     "area", cols.get(3).text(),
142:                     "price", cols.get(4).text()
143:             );
144:             parsed.add(new RawImportRecord("html-row-" + i, fields, row.outerHtml()));
145:             i++;
146:         }
147:         return parsed;
148:     }
149: 
150:     private void cleanAndPersist(Long jobId, RawImportRecord raw, List<String> notes) {
151:         Map<String, String> map = raw.fields();
152: 
153:         String stop = mapped(map, "stop", "stopName", "station_name");
154:         String address = mapped(map, "address", "addr", "location");
155:         String apartment = mapped(map, "apartment", "aptType", "house_type");
156:         String area = mapped(map, "area", "area_size", "size");
157:         String price = mapped(map, "price", "rent", "monthly_price");
158: 
159:         String cleanStop = normalizeOrNull("stop_name", stop, raw.sourceRef(), jobId, notes);
160:         String cleanAddress = normalizeOrNull("address", address, raw.sourceRef(), jobId, notes);
161:         String cleanApartment = normalizeOrNull("apartment_type", apartment, raw.sourceRef(), jobId, notes);
162:         String cleanArea = normalizeArea(area, raw.sourceRef(), jobId, notes);
163:         String cleanPrice = normalizePrice(price, raw.sourceRef(), jobId, notes);
164: 
165:         CleanedRecordEntity entity = new CleanedRecordEntity();
166:         entity.setImportJobId(jobId);
167:         entity.setSourceRef(raw.sourceRef());
168:         entity.setStopName(cleanStop);
169:         entity.setAddress(cleanAddress);
170:         entity.setApartmentType(cleanApartment);
171:         entity.setAreaStandardized(cleanArea);
172:         entity.setPriceStandardized(cleanPrice);
173:         entity.setRawSnapshot(raw.rawSnapshot());
174:         cleanedRecordRepository.save(entity);
175: 
176:         versionFieldIfChanged(cleanStop, "address", cleanAddress, jobId);
177:         versionFieldIfChanged(cleanStop, "apartment_type", cleanApartment, jobId);
178:         versionFieldIfChanged(cleanStop, "area_standardized", cleanArea, jobId);
179:         versionFieldIfChanged(cleanStop, "price_standardized", cleanPrice, jobId);
180:     }
181: 
182:     private void versionFieldIfChanged(String stopName, String fieldName, String newValue, Long jobId) {
183:         Optional<StopStructureVersionEntity> latest = stopStructureVersionRepository
184:                 .findTopByStopNameAndFieldNameOrderByVersionNumberDesc(stopName, fieldName);
185:         String old = latest.map(StopStructureVersionEntity::getNewValue).orElse("NULL");
186:         if (old.equals(newValue)) {
187:             return;
188:         }
189:         StopStructureVersionEntity version = new StopStructureVersionEntity();
190:         version.setStopName(stopName);
191:         version.setFieldName(fieldName);
192:         version.setOldValue(old);
193:         version.setNewValue(newValue);
194:         version.setVersionNumber(latest.map(v -> v.getVersionNumber() + 1).orElse(1));
195:         version.setImportJobId(jobId);
196:         stopStructureVersionRepository.save(version);
197:     }
198: 
199:     private String mapped(Map<String, String> map, String... keys) {
200:         for (String key : keys) {
201:             String value = map.get(key);
202:             if (value != null && !value.isBlank()) {
203:                 return value;
204:             }
205:         }
206:         return null;
207:     }
208: 
209:     private String normalizeOrNull(String fieldName, String value, String sourceRef, Long jobId, List<String> notes) {
210:         String missingMarker = adminControlService.getCleaningRules().missingValueMarker();
211:         boolean trimEnabled = adminControlService.getCleaningRules().trimEnabled();
212:         if (value == null || value.isBlank()) {
213:             audit(jobId, sourceRef, fieldName, "", missingMarker, "MISSING_TO_NULL");
214:             notes.add("Missing value mapped to " + missingMarker + " for " + fieldName + " from " + sourceRef);
215:             return missingMarker;
216:         }
217:         String cleaned = trimEnabled ? value.trim() : value;
218:         if (!cleaned.equals(value)) {
219:             audit(jobId, sourceRef, fieldName, value, cleaned, "TRIMMED");
220:         }
221:         return cleaned;
222:     }
223: 
224:     private String normalizeArea(String value, String sourceRef, Long jobId, List<String> notes) {
225:         String areaUnit = adminControlService.getCleaningRules().areaUnit();
226:         String missingMarker = adminControlService.getCleaningRules().missingValueMarker();
227:         if (value == null || value.isBlank()) {
228:             audit(jobId, sourceRef, "area", "", missingMarker, "MISSING_TO_NULL");
229:             notes.add("Missing area mapped to " + missingMarker + " from " + sourceRef);
230:             return missingMarker;
231:         }
232:         String numeric = extractFirstNumber(value);
233:         String cleaned = numeric == null ? missingMarker : numeric + " " + areaUnit;
234:         audit(jobId, sourceRef, "area", value, cleaned, "UNIT_STANDARDIZED");
235:         return cleaned;
236:     }
237: 
238:     private String normalizePrice(String value, String sourceRef, Long jobId, List<String> notes) {
239:         String priceUnit = adminControlService.getCleaningRules().priceUnit();
240:         String missingMarker = adminControlService.getCleaningRules().missingValueMarker();
241:         if (value == null || value.isBlank()) {
242:             audit(jobId, sourceRef, "price", "", missingMarker, "MISSING_TO_NULL");
243:             notes.add("Missing price mapped to " + missingMarker + " from " + sourceRef);
244:             return missingMarker;
245:         }
246:         String numeric = extractFirstNumber(value);
247:         String cleaned = numeric == null ? missingMarker : numeric + " " + priceUnit;
248:         audit(jobId, sourceRef, "price", value, cleaned, "UNIT_STANDARDIZED");
249:         return cleaned;
250:     }
251: 
252:     private String extractFirstNumber(String value) {
253:         if (value == null) {
254:             return null;
255:         }
256:         Matcher matcher = FIRST_NUMBER.matcher(value.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT));
257:         if (!matcher.find()) {
258:             return null;
259:         }
260:         return matcher.group();
261:     }
262: 
263:     private void audit(Long jobId, String sourceRef, String fieldName, String raw, String cleaned, String action) {
264:         CleaningAuditEntity log = new CleaningAuditEntity();
265:         log.setImportJobId(jobId);
266:         log.setSourceRef(sourceRef);
267:         log.setFieldName(fieldName);
268:         log.setRawValue(raw == null ? "" : raw);
269:         log.setCleanedValue(cleaned == null ? "" : cleaned);
270:         log.setAction(action);
271:         cleaningAuditRepository.save(log);
272:     }
273: 
274:     private ImportJobResponse toJobResponse(ImportJobEntity job) {
275:         return new ImportJobResponse(
276:                 job.getId(),
277:                 job.getStatus(),
278:                 job.getSourceName(),
279:                 job.getTotalRows(),
280:                 job.getSuccessRows(),
281:                 job.getFailedRows(),
282:                 job.getCreatedAt(),
283:                 job.getUpdatedAt()
284:         );
285:     }
286: }
