7: import org.springframework.scheduling.annotation.Scheduled;
8: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
9: import org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;
10: 
11: import java.time.Instant;
12: import java.time.LocalTime;
13: import java.time.ZoneId;
14: import java.util.Optional;
15: 
16: @Service
17: public class MessageSchedulerService {
18: 
19:     private final BookingEventRepository bookingRepository;
20:     private final UserRepository userRepository;
21:     private final MessageQueueService queueService;
22:     private final NotificationRuleService notificationRuleService;
23: 
24:     public MessageSchedulerService(BookingEventRepository bookingRepository,
25:                                    UserRepository userRepository,
26:                                    MessageQueueService queueService,
27:                                    NotificationRuleService notificationRuleService) {
28:         this.bookingRepository = bookingRepository;
29:         this.userRepository = userRepository;
30:         this.queueService = queueService;
31:         this.notificationRuleService = notificationRuleService;
32:     }
33: 
34:     @Scheduled(fixedDelayString = "${app.message.scheduler-ms:60000}")
35:     @Transactional
36:     public void generateReservationSuccessMessages() {
37:         for (BookingEventEntity booking : bookingRepository.findByReservationSuccessSentFalse()) {
38:             queueService.enqueue(
39:                     booking.getUsername(),
40:                     MessageType.RESERVATION_SUCCESS,
41:                     "Reservation Confirmed",
42:                     "Reservation for route " + booking.getRouteNumber() + " confirmed. Reference token " + shortToken(booking.getPassengerPhoneToken())
43:             );
44:             booking.setReservationSuccessSent(true);
45:             bookingRepository.save(booking);
46:         }
47:     }
48: 
49:     @Scheduled(fixedDelayString = "${app.message.scheduler-ms:60000}")
50:     @Transactional
51:     public void generateArrivalReminders() {
52:         Instant now = Instant.now();
53:         for (BookingEventEntity booking : bookingRepository.findByArrivalReminderSentFalseAndStartTimeBefore(now.plusSeconds(3600))) {
54:             Optional<UserEntity> userOpt = userRepository.findByUsername(booking.getUsername());
55:             if (userOpt.isEmpty()) {
56:                 continue;
57:             }
58:             UserEntity user = userOpt.get();
59:             Instant trigger = booking.getStartTime().minusSeconds((long) user.getReminderLeadMinutes() * 60L);
60:             if (now.isBefore(trigger)) {
61:                 continue;
62:             }
63:             LocalTime currentLocal = LocalTime.ofInstant(now, ZoneId.systemDefault());
64:             boolean allowed = notificationRuleService.isNotificationAllowed(user, NotificationType.ARRIVAL_REMINDER, currentLocal);
65:             if (allowed) {
66:                 queueService.enqueue(
67:                         booking.getUsername(),
68:                         MessageType.ARRIVAL_REMINDER,
69:                         "Arrival Reminder",
70:                         "Your bus on route " + booking.getRouteNumber() + " arrives in " + user.getReminderLeadMinutes() + " minutes. Ref " + shortToken(booking.getPassengerPhoneToken())
71:                 );
72:             }
73:             booking.setArrivalReminderSent(true);
74:             bookingRepository.save(booking);
75:         }
76:     }
77: 
78:     @Scheduled(fixedDelayString = "${app.message.scheduler-ms:60000}")
79:     @Transactional
80:     public void generateMissedCheckIns() {
81:         Instant now = Instant.now().minusSeconds(300);
82:         for (BookingEventEntity booking : bookingRepository.findByMissedCheckInSentFalseAndStartTimeBefore(now)) {
83:             queueService.enqueue(
84:                     booking.getUsername(),
85:                     MessageType.MISSED_CHECK_IN,
86:                     "Missed Check-In",
87:                     "You missed check-in for route " + booking.getRouteNumber() + ". Please rebook if needed."
88:             );
89:             booking.setMissedCheckInSent(true);
90:             bookingRepository.save(booking);
91:         }
92:     }
93: 
94:     private String shortToken(String token) {
95:         if (token == null || token.length() < 8) {
96:             return "n/a";
97:         }
98:         return token.substring(0, 8);
99:     }
100: }

<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.monitoring;
2: 
3: import com.mindflow.security.messagecenter.MessageQueueEventRepository;
4: import com.mindflow.security.messagecenter.QueueStatus;
5: import io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry;
6: import io.micrometer.core.instrument.Timer;
7: import io.micrometer.core.instrument.distribution.ValueAtPercentile;
8: import org.slf4j.Logger;
9: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
10: import org.springframework.scheduling.annotation.Scheduled;
11: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
12: import org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;
13: 
14: import java.time.Instant;
15: import java.util.List;
16: import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;
17: 
18: @Service
19: public class ObservabilityService {
20: 
21:     private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ObservabilityService.class);
22:     private static final long MAX_REASONABLE_P95_MS = 300_000;
23: 
24:     private final MeterRegistry meterRegistry;
25:     private final MessageQueueEventRepository queueEventRepository;
26:     private final AlertDiagnosticRepository alertRepository;
27:     private final MonitoringProperties properties;
28: 
29:     public ObservabilityService(MeterRegistry meterRegistry,
30:                                 MessageQueueEventRepository queueEventRepository,
31:                                 AlertDiagnosticRepository alertRepository,
32:                                 MonitoringProperties properties) {
33:         this.meterRegistry = meterRegistry;
34:         this.queueEventRepository = queueEventRepository;
35:         this.alertRepository = alertRepository;
36:         this.properties = properties;
37:     }
38: 
39:     public void recordWorkflowLog(String workflow, String detail) {
40:         String trace = TraceContext.getTraceId();
41:         log.info("event=workflow activity={} traceId={} detail={}", workflow, trace, detail);
42:     }
43: 
44:     @Scheduled(fixedDelayString = "${app.monitoring.check-ms:30000}")
45:     @Transactional
46:     public void evaluateAlerts() {
47:         int backlog = queueEventRepository.findTop100ByStatusOrderByCreatedAtAsc(QueueStatus.PENDING).size();
48:         meterRegistry.gauge("app.queue.backlog", backlog);
49: 
50:         if (backlog > properties.getQueueBacklogThreshold()) {
51:             createAlert("QUEUE_BACKLOG", "WARN",
52:                     "Queue backlog is " + backlog + " which exceeds threshold " + properties.getQueueBacklogThreshold());
53:         }
54: 
55:         Timer timer = meterRegistry.find("app.api.request.duration").timer();
56:         if (timer != null) {
57:             long p95Ms = Math.round(extractPercentileMs(timer, 0.95));
58:             if (p95Ms > MAX_REASONABLE_P95_MS) {
59:                 log.warn("event=alert-skip type=API_P95 reason=unrealistic_value valueMs={}", p95Ms);
60:                 return;
61:             }
62:             if (p95Ms > properties.getApiP95ThresholdMs()) {
63:                 createAlert("API_P95", "WARN",
64:                         "API p95 latency is " + p95Ms + "ms which exceeds threshold " + properties.getApiP95ThresholdMs() + "ms");
65:             }
66:         }
67:     }
68: 
69:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
70:     public List<AlertDiagnosticEntity> recentDiagnostics() {
71:         return alertRepository.findTop100ByOrderByCreatedAtDesc();
72:     }
73: 
74:     private void createAlert(String type, String severity, String message) {
75:         AlertDiagnosticEntity alert = new AlertDiagnosticEntity();
76:         alert.setAlertType(type);
77:         alert.setSeverity(severity);
78:         alert.setMessage(message + " @ " + Instant.now());
79:         alertRepository.save(alert);
80:         log.warn("event=alert type={} severity={} message={}", type, severity, message);
81:     }
82: 
83:     private double extractPercentileMs(Timer timer, double percentile) {
84:         ValueAtPercentile[] values = timer.takeSnapshot().percentileValues();
85:         if (values.length == 0) {
86:             return 0;
87:         }
88:         for (ValueAtPercentile candidate : values) {
89:             if (Math.abs(candidate.percentile() - percentile) < 0.001) {
90:                 return toMillis(candidate.value(), timer.baseTimeUnit());
91:             }
92:         }
93:         return toMillis(values[values.length - 1].value(), timer.baseTimeUnit());
94:     }
95: 
96:     static double toMillis(double value, TimeUnit baseUnit) {
97:         return switch (baseUnit) {
98:             case NANOSECONDS -> value / 1_000_000d;
99:             case MICROSECONDS -> value / 1_000d;
100:             case MILLISECONDS -> value;
101:             case SECONDS -> value * 1_000d;
102:             case MINUTES -> value * 60_000d;
103:             case HOURS -> value * 3_600_000d;
104:             case DAYS -> value * 86_400_000d;
105:         };
106:     }
107: }

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ROOT_DIR="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
BACKEND_DIR="${ROOT_DIR}/backend"
ERROR_LOG="${ROOT_DIR}/unit_tests/error.log"
> "$ERROR_LOG"

TEST_CLASSES=(
  "MessagePrivacyServiceTest"
  "SearchServiceTest"
  "NotificationRuleServiceTest"
  "RbacAccessTest"
  "ObservabilityServiceTest"
  "WorkflowAuthorizationTest"
  "DataImportServiceTest"
  "ApiSecuritySemanticsTest"
)

TOTAL=${#TEST_CLASSES[@]}
PASS=0
FAIL=0

TEST_ARG=$(IFS=,; echo "${TEST_CLASSES[*]}")

run_maven_local() {
  bash -lc "cd \"${BACKEND_DIR}\" && mvn -q -Dtest=${TEST_ARG} test"
}

run_maven_docker() {
  local m2_volume="mindflow-m2-cache"
  bash -lc "MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1 docker run --rm -v \"${BACKEND_DIR}:/app\" -v \"${m2_volume}:/root/.m2\" -w \"/app\" maven:3.9.8-eclipse-temurin-17-alpine mvn -q -Dtest=${TEST_ARG} test"
}

echo "[UNIT] Running ${TOTAL} tests in one Maven invocation"
echo "[UNIT] Targets: ${TEST_ARG}"

if command -v mvn >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if run_maven_local; then
    PASS=$TOTAL
  else
    FAIL=$TOTAL
    echo "[UNIT][FAIL] Maven local run failed" >> "$ERROR_LOG"
  fi
elif command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if run_maven_docker; then
    PASS=$TOTAL
  else
    FAIL=$TOTAL
    echo "[UNIT][FAIL] Maven docker run failed" >> "$ERROR_LOG"
  fi
else
  FAIL=$TOTAL
  echo "[UNIT] neither mvn nor docker found in environment" | tee -a "$ERROR_LOG"
fi

echo "UNIT_SUMMARY total=${TOTAL} pass=${PASS} fail=${FAIL}"
if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Failure logs: ${ERROR_LOG}"
  exit 1
fi

<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.workflow;
2: 
3: import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
4: import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Autowired;
5: import org.springframework.boot.test.context.SpringBootTest;
6: import org.springframework.scheduling.annotation.Scheduled;
7: import org.springframework.test.context.ActiveProfiles;
8: 
9: import java.time.Instant;
10: import java.util.List;
11: 
12: import static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;
13: 
14: @SpringBootTest
15: @ActiveProfiles("test")
16: class WorkflowEscalationTest {
17: 
18:     @Autowired
19:     private WorkflowTaskRepository repository;
20: 
21:     @Autowired
22:     private WorkflowService workflowService;
23: 
24:     @Test
25:     void evaluateEscalationsMarksOnlyStaleActionableTasks() {
26:         WorkflowTaskEntity staleSubmitted = saveTask(WorkflowStatus.SUBMITTED, Instant.now().minusSeconds(25 * 3600L));
27:         WorkflowTaskEntity staleInReview = saveTask(WorkflowStatus.IN_REVIEW, Instant.now().minusSeconds(26 * 3600L));
28:         WorkflowTaskEntity freshSubmitted = saveTask(WorkflowStatus.SUBMITTED, Instant.now().minusSeconds(2 * 3600L));
29:         WorkflowTaskEntity staleApproved = saveTask(WorkflowStatus.APPROVED, Instant.now().minusSeconds(30 * 3600L));
30: 
31:         List<WorkflowTaskResponse> escalated = workflowService.evaluateEscalations();
32: 
33:         assertThat(escalated)
34:                 .extracting(WorkflowTaskResponse::id)
35:                 .contains(staleSubmitted.getId(), staleInReview.getId())
36:                 .doesNotContain(freshSubmitted.getId(), staleApproved.getId());
37: 
38:         assertThat(repository.findById(staleSubmitted.getId())).get().extracting(WorkflowTaskEntity::isEscalated).isEqualTo(true);
39:         assertThat(repository.findById(staleInReview.getId())).get().extracting(WorkflowTaskEntity::isEscalated).isEqualTo(true);
40:         assertThat(repository.findById(freshSubmitted.getId())).get().extracting(WorkflowTaskEntity::isEscalated).isEqualTo(false);
41:         assertThat(repository.findById(staleApproved.getId())).get().extracting(WorkflowTaskEntity::isEscalated).isEqualTo(false);
42:     }
43: 
44:     @Test
45:     void escalationSchedulerIsConfiguredWithScheduledAnnotation() throws Exception {
46:         Scheduled scheduled = WorkflowEscalationScheduler.class
47:                 .getDeclaredMethod("evaluateStaleTasks")
48:                 .getAnnotation(Scheduled.class);
49: 
50:         assertThat(scheduled).isNotNull();
51:         assertThat(scheduled.fixedDelayString()).isEqualTo("${app.workflow.escalation-check-ms:60000}");
52:     }
53: 
54:     private WorkflowTaskEntity saveTask(WorkflowStatus status, Instant lastActionAt) {
55:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = new WorkflowTaskEntity();
56:         task.setType(WorkflowType.ROUTE_DATA_CHANGE);
57:         task.setMode(WorkflowMode.CONDITIONAL);
58:         task.setStatus(status);
59:         task.setTitle("Escalation test");
60:         task.setPayload("{}");
61:         task.setSubmittedBy("dispatcher_a");
62:         task.setAssignedTo("dispatcher_a");
63:         task.setCollaborators("dispatcher_a");
64:         task.setCurrentStep(1);
65:         task.setTotalSteps(3);
66:         task.setRequiredApprovals(1);
67:         task.setReceivedApprovals(0);
68:         task.setEscalated(false);
69:         task.setLastActionAt(lastActionAt);
70:         return repository.save(task);
71:     }
72: }

content.text:
Implemented a focused gap-fill pass based on `.tmp/self-test_report.md`, targeting the highest-priority testing/automation gaps and wiring one missing automation behavior.

- Added automatic workflow timeout escalation scheduling so stale tasks are evaluated without manual API calls via `fullstack/backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowEscalationScheduler.java`.
- Added message-center ownership authorization tests (read/delete as non-owner must be `403`, owner actions succeed) in `fullstack/backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageCenterAuthorizationTest.java`.
- Added workflow escalation behavior tests (only stale actionable statuses escalate, finalized/fresh tasks do not) plus scheduler annotation verification in `fullstack/backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowEscalationTest.java`.
- Added notification preferences controller validation tests for bad inputs (`400` cases) in `fullstack/backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/notification/NotificationSettingsControllerTest.java`.
- Updated default unit runner to include missing and new tests in `fullstack/unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh` (now includes `AuthControllerTest` and the new coverage classes).
- Cleared the transient failure marker in `fullstack/unit_tests/error.log` after the script attempt.

Test run status:
- I executed `bash fullstack/unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh`.
- It failed due to environment/runtime tooling, not test logic: Docker daemon was unavailable (`dockerDesktopLinuxEngine` pipe missing), and this script falls back to Docker when local Maven is absent.

If you want, next I can:
1. Extend API tests for workflow escalation endpoint and message ownership paths in `fullstack/API_tests/run_api_tests.sh`.
2. Add a small frontend spec set for login + route gating + message center interactions.
3. Update `.tmp/self-test_report.md` with a ΓÇ£gap remediation completedΓÇ¥ section reflecting these exact changes.
Have all potential gaps been resolved?

Not yetΓÇöimportant gaps are reduced, but not fully resolved.

WhatΓÇÖs now addressed:
- Automatic workflow escalation trigger added (`WorkflowEscalationScheduler`).
- Message-center ownership tests added.
- Notification preference controller validation tests added.
- Workflow escalation tests added.
- Unit test runner now includes previously omitted `AuthControllerTest` plus new tests.

What still remains (from the original high/medium risk list):
- Import/admin-managed data still does not feed passenger search runtime path.
- Admin templates/dictionaries are still mostly CRUD and not applied in runtime message generation/import mapping.
- Sensitivity-level desensitization is still not integrated into the real inbox pipeline (separate service exists, but pipeline metadata/use is incomplete).
- Frontend route guards are still missing.
- Observability alert-trigger tests are still limited (no robust backlog/P95 trigger assertions).
- Import coverage still lacks full HTML/audit/versioning depth.
- Frontend test coverage is still effectively absent.

So: **partial remediation completed, full closure not yet**.
Are the remaining gaps feasible to implement?

YesΓÇöthese remaining gaps are all feasible in this codebase.

- **High feasibility (small/medium effort):** frontend route guards, observability alert-trigger tests, broader import tests (HTML/audit/versioning), API test expansion.
- **Medium feasibility (medium effort):** wiring admin templates/dictionaries into runtime message generation and import mapping.
- **Medium-high feasibility (larger but straightforward):** connecting import/canonical stop data to passenger search (schema + service wiring + migration/backfill logic + tests).
- **Medium feasibility (medium effort):** integrating sensitivity-level metadata through queue/inbox pipeline and applying level-based masking consistently.

Nothing looks blocked by architecture; itΓÇÖs mostly implementation and test coverage work. If you want, I can do this in a prioritized sequence so each step is independently shippable.
Sounds goodΓÇögo ahead. A prioritized, step-by-step approach with independently shippable increments works well.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    role VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (role IN ('PASSENGER', 'DISPATCHER', 'ADMIN')),
    enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    arrival_reminder_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    reservation_success_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    reminder_lead_minutes INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 10,
    dnd_start TIME,
    dnd_end TIME,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workflow_tasks (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    type VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('ROUTE_DATA_CHANGE', 'REMINDER_CONFIGURATION', 'ABNORMAL_DATA_REVIEW')),
    mode VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (mode IN ('CONDITIONAL', 'JOINT', 'PARALLEL')),
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('SUBMITTED', 'IN_REVIEW', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED', 'RETURNED')),

<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { Injectable, signal } from '@angular/core';
2: import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http';
3: import { Observable, tap } from 'rxjs';
4: import { environment } from '../environments/environment';
5: import { LoginRequest, LoginResponse } from './auth.models';
6: 
7: const TOKEN_KEY = 'mindflow_token';
8: const ROLE_KEY = 'mindflow_role';
9: const USER_KEY = 'mindflow_user';
10: 
11: @Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' })
12: export class AuthService {
13:   readonly token = signal<string | null>(localStorage.getItem(TOKEN_KEY));
14:   readonly role = signal<string | null>(localStorage.getItem(ROLE_KEY));
15:   readonly username = signal<string | null>(localStorage.getItem(USER_KEY));
16: 
17:   constructor(private readonly http: HttpClient) {}
18: 
19:   login(payload: LoginRequest): Observable<LoginResponse> {
20:     return this.http
21:       .post<LoginResponse>(`${environment.apiBaseUrl}/auth/login`, payload)
22:       .pipe(tap((res) => this.persistAuth(res)));
23:   }
24: 
25:   logout(): void {
26:     localStorage.removeItem(TOKEN_KEY);
27:     localStorage.removeItem(ROLE_KEY);
28:     localStorage.removeItem(USER_KEY);
29:     this.token.set(null);
30:     this.role.set(null);
31:     this.username.set(null);
32:   }
33: 
34:   private persistAuth(response: LoginResponse): void {
35:     localStorage.setItem(TOKEN_KEY, response.token);
36:     localStorage.setItem(ROLE_KEY, response.role);
37:     localStorage.setItem(USER_KEY, response.username);
38:     this.token.set(response.token);
39:     this.role.set(response.role);
40:     this.username.set(response.username);
41:   }
42: }

(End of file - total 42 lines)

35:     public void enqueue(String username, MessageType type, String title, String content) {
36:         MessageQueueEventEntity event = new MessageQueueEventEntity();
37:         event.setUsername(username);
38:         event.setType(type);
39:         event.setTitle(title);
40:         event.setContent(messageMaskingService.mask(content));
41:         event.setTraceId(TraceContext.getTraceId());
42:         event.setStatus(QueueStatus.PENDING);
43:         queueRepository.save(event);
44:     }
45: 
46:     @Scheduled(fixedDelayString = "${app.message.queue-process-ms:10000}")
47:     @Transactional
48:     public void processQueue() {
49:         Timer.Sample sample = Timer.start(meterRegistry);
50:         List<MessageQueueEventEntity> events = queueRepository.findTop100ByStatusOrderByCreatedAtAsc(QueueStatus.PENDING);
51:         for (MessageQueueEventEntity event : events) {
52:             MessageEntity message = new MessageEntity();
53:             message.setUsername(event.getUsername());
54:             message.setType(event.getType());
55:             message.setTitle(event.getTitle());
56:             message.setContent(event.getContent());
57:             message.setTraceId(event.getTraceId());
58:             message.setRead(false);
59:             message.setMasked(false);
60:             messageRepository.save(message);
61: 
62:             event.setStatus(QueueStatus.PROCESSED);
63:             queueRepository.save(event);
64:         }
65:         sample.stop(Timer.builder("app.workflow.queue.duration")
66:                 .publishPercentiles(0.5, 0.95, 0.99)
67:                 .register(meterRegistry));
68:         observabilityService.recordWorkflowLog("queue", "processed=" + events.size());
69:     }
70: }

<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.integration;
2: 
3: import jakarta.validation.Valid;
4: import jakarta.validation.constraints.Positive;
5: import org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;
6: import org.springframework.security.access.prepost.PreAuthorize;
7: import org.springframework.validation.annotation.Validated;
8: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;
9: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PostMapping;
10: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestBody;
11: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;
12: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestParam;
13: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;
14: 
15: import java.util.List;
16: 
17: @RestController
18: @RequestMapping("/api/admin/integration")
19: @Validated
20: public class DataIntegrationController {
21: 
22:     private final DataImportService dataImportService;
23:     private final CleaningAuditRepository cleaningAuditRepository;
24:     private final StopStructureVersionRepository stopStructureVersionRepository;
25: 
26:     public DataIntegrationController(DataImportService dataImportService,
27:                                      CleaningAuditRepository cleaningAuditRepository,
28:                                      StopStructureVersionRepository stopStructureVersionRepository) {
29:         this.dataImportService = dataImportService;
30:         this.cleaningAuditRepository = cleaningAuditRepository;
31:         this.stopStructureVersionRepository = stopStructureVersionRepository;
32:     }
33: 
34:     @PostMapping("/import")
35:     @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')")
36:     public ResponseEntity<ImportSummaryResponse> importData(@Valid @RequestBody ImportRequest request) {
37:         return ResponseEntity.ok(dataImportService.ingest(request));
38:     }
39: 
40:     @GetMapping("/jobs")
41:     @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')")
42:     public ResponseEntity<List<ImportJobResponse>> jobs() {
43:         return ResponseEntity.ok(dataImportService.listJobs());
44:     }
45: 
46:     @GetMapping("/audit")
47:     @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')")
48:     public ResponseEntity<List<CleaningAuditResponse>> audit(@RequestParam("jobId") @Positive Long jobId) {
49:         List<CleaningAuditResponse> rows = cleaningAuditRepository.findByImportJobIdOrderByIdAsc(jobId)
50:                 .stream()
51:                 .map(a -> new CleaningAuditResponse(
52:                         a.getSourceRef(),
53:                         a.getFieldName(),
54:                         a.getRawValue(),
55:                         a.getCleanedValue(),
56:                         a.getAction()))
57:                 .toList();
58:         return ResponseEntity.ok(rows);
59:     }
60: 
61:     @GetMapping("/versions")
62:     @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')")
63:     public ResponseEntity<List<StopVersionResponse>> versions(
64:             @RequestParam(name = "stopName", required = false) String stopName,
65:             @RequestParam(name = "jobId", required = false) @Positive Long jobId) {
66:         List<StopStructureVersionEntity> rows;
67:         if (stopName != null && !stopName.isBlank()) {
68:             rows = stopStructureVersionRepository.findByStopNameOrderByVersionNumberDesc(stopName);
69:         } else if (jobId != null) {
70:             rows = stopStructureVersionRepository.findByImportJobIdOrderByIdAsc(jobId);
71:         } else {
72:             rows = stopStructureVersionRepository.findAll();
73:         }
74:         return ResponseEntity.ok(rows.stream()
75:                 .map(v -> new StopVersionResponse(
76:                         v.getStopName(),
77:                         v.getFieldName(),
78:                         v.getOldValue(),
79:                         v.getNewValue(),
80:                         v.getVersionNumber(),
81:                         v.getImportJobId()))
82:                 .toList());
83:     }
84: }

<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.message;
2: 
3: import com.mindflow.security.user.Role;
4: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
5: 
6: @Service
7: public class MessagePrivacyService {
8: 
9:     public String desensitize(String message, SensitivityLevel sensitivityLevel, Role role) {
10:         if (message == null || message.isBlank()) {
11:             return message;
12:         }
13:         if (sensitivityLevel == null) {
14:             sensitivityLevel = SensitivityLevel.LOW;
15:         }
16: 
17:         return switch (sensitivityLevel) {
18:             case LOW -> message;
19:             case MEDIUM -> role == Role.PASSENGER ? partiallyMask(message) : message;
20:             case HIGH -> {
21:                 if (role == Role.ADMIN) {
22:                     yield message;
23:                 }
24:                 yield "[REDACTED]";
25:             }
26:         };
27:     }
28: 
29:     private String partiallyMask(String value) {
30:         if (value.length() <= 4) {
31:             return "****";
32:         }
33:         int keep = Math.max(2, value.length() / 4);
34:         int maskCount = value.length() - (keep * 2);
35:         if (maskCount < 1) {
36:             maskCount = 1;
37:         }
38: 
39:         String prefix = value.substring(0, keep);
40:         String suffix = value.substring(value.length() - keep);
41:         return prefix + "*".repeat(maskCount) + suffix;
42:     }
43: }

<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.message;
2: 
3: import com.mindflow.security.user.Role;
4: import jakarta.validation.Valid;
5: import org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;
6: import org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;
7: import org.springframework.security.core.Authentication;
8: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PostMapping;
9: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestBody;
10: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;
11: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;
12: import org.springframework.web.server.ResponseStatusException;
13: 
14: @RestController
15: @RequestMapping("/api/passenger/messages")
16: public class MessageController {
17: 
18:     private final MessagePrivacyService messagePrivacyService;
19: 
20:     public MessageController(MessagePrivacyService messagePrivacyService) {
21:         this.messagePrivacyService = messagePrivacyService;
22:     }
23: 
24:     @PostMapping("/desensitize")
25:     public ResponseEntity<MessageResponse> desensitize(@Valid @RequestBody MessagePayload payload,
26:                                                        Authentication authentication) {
27:         String roleAuthority = authentication.getAuthorities().stream()
28:                 .findFirst()
29:                 .orElseThrow(() -> new ResponseStatusException(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, "No role found"))
30:                 .getAuthority();
31: 
32:         Role role = Role.valueOf(roleAuthority.replace("ROLE_", ""));
33:         SensitivityLevel level = payload.sensitivityLevel() == null ? SensitivityLevel.LOW : payload.sensitivityLevel();
34:         String masked = messagePrivacyService.desensitize(payload.content(), level, role);
35: 
36:         return ResponseEntity.ok(new MessageResponse(payload.content(), masked, level, role.name()));
37:     }
38: }

<content>1: import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';
2: import { HttpClient } from '@angular/common/http';
3: import { Observable } from 'rxjs';
4: import { DictionaryItem, RuleWeights, TemplateConfig } from './auth.models';
5: 
6: @Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' })
7: export class AdminControlService {
8:   constructor(private readonly http: HttpClient) {}
9: 
10:   getWeights(): Observable<RuleWeights> {
11:     return this.http.get<RuleWeights>('/api/admin/control/search-weights');
12:   }
13: 
14:   updateWeights(payload: RuleWeights): Observable<RuleWeights> {
15:     return this.http.put<RuleWeights>('/api/admin/control/search-weights', payload);
16:   }
17: 
18:   listTemplates(): Observable<TemplateConfig[]> {
19:     return this.http.get<TemplateConfig[]>('/api/admin/control/templates');
20:   }
21: 
22:   createTemplate(payload: Omit<TemplateConfig, 'id'>): Observable<TemplateConfig> {
23:     return this.http.post<TemplateConfig>('/api/admin/control/templates', payload);
24:   }
25: 
26:   updateTemplate(id: number, payload: Omit<TemplateConfig, 'id'>): Observable<TemplateConfig> {
27:     return this.http.put<TemplateConfig>(`/api/admin/control/templates/${id}`, payload);
28:   }
29: 
30:   deleteTemplate(id: number): Observable<void> {
31:     return this.http.delete<void>(`/api/admin/control/templates/${id}`);
32:   }
33: 
34:   listDictionary(category?: string): Observable<DictionaryItem[]> {
35:     if (category) {
36:       return this.http.get<DictionaryItem[]>('/api/admin/control/dictionaries', { params: { category } });
37:     }
38:     return this.http.get<DictionaryItem[]>('/api/admin/control/dictionaries');
39:   }
40: 
41:   createDictionary(payload: Omit<DictionaryItem, 'id'>): Observable<DictionaryItem> {
42:     return this.http.post<DictionaryItem>('/api/admin/control/dictionaries', payload);
43:   }
44: 
45:   updateDictionary(id: number, payload: Omit<DictionaryItem, 'id'>): Observable<DictionaryItem> {
46:     return this.http.put<DictionaryItem>(`/api/admin/control/dictionaries/${id}`, payload);
47:   }
48: 
49:   deleteDictionary(id: number): Observable<void> {
50:     return this.http.delete<void>(`/api/admin/control/dictionaries/${id}`);
51:   }
52: }

<path>C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\mindFlow\TASK-20260318-393C91\fullstack\backend\src\main\java\com\mindflow\security\config\SecurityConfig.java</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.config;
2: 
3: import com.mindflow.security.auth.JwtAuthenticationFilter;
4: import com.mindflow.security.monitoring.TraceContext;
5: import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper;
6: import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
7: import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
8: import org.springframework.security.config.Customizer;
9: import org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationManager;
10: import org.springframework.security.authentication.dao.DaoAuthenticationProvider;
11: import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.authentication.configuration.AuthenticationConfiguration;
12: import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.method.configuration.EnableMethodSecurity;
13: import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.builders.HttpSecurity;
14: import org.springframework.security.config.http.SessionCreationPolicy;
15: import org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetailsService;
16: import org.springframework.security.crypto.bcrypt.BCryptPasswordEncoder;
17: import org.springframework.security.crypto.password.PasswordEncoder;
18: import org.springframework.security.web.SecurityFilterChain;
19: import org.springframework.security.web.authentication.UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter;
20: 
21: import java.time.Instant;
22: import java.util.Map;
23: 
24: @Configuration
25: @EnableMethodSecurity
26: public class SecurityConfig {
27: 
28:     private final JwtAuthenticationFilter jwtAuthenticationFilter;
29:     private final UserDetailsService userDetailsService;
30: 
31:     public SecurityConfig(JwtAuthenticationFilter jwtAuthenticationFilter, UserDetailsService userDetailsService) {
32:         this.jwtAuthenticationFilter = jwtAuthenticationFilter;
33:         this.userDetailsService = userDetailsService;
34:     }
35: 
36:     @Bean
37:     public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
38:         http
39:                 .cors(Customizer.withDefaults())
40:                 .csrf(csrf -> csrf.disable())
41:                 .sessionManagement(session -> session.sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS))
42:                 .exceptionHandling(ex -> ex
43:                         .authenticationEntryPoint((request, response, authException) -> {
44:                             response.setStatus(401);
45:                             response.setContentType("application/json");
46:                             response.getWriter().write(new ObjectMapper().writeValueAsString(Map.of(
47:                                     "code", 401,
48:                                     "msg", "Unauthorized",
49:                                     "traceId", TraceContext.getTraceId(),
50:                                     "timestamp", Instant.now().toString()
51:                             )));
52:                         })
53:                         .accessDeniedHandler((request, response, accessDeniedException) -> {
54:                             response.setStatus(403);
55:                             response.setContentType("application/json");
56:                             response.getWriter().write(new ObjectMapper().writeValueAsString(Map.of(
57:                                     "code", 403,
58:                                     "msg", "Access denied",
59:                                     "traceId", TraceContext.getTraceId(),
60:                                     "timestamp", Instant.now().toString()
61:                             )));
62:                         })
63:                 )
64:                 .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
65:                         .requestMatchers("/api/auth/login", "/", "/index.html", "/favicon.ico", "/actuator/health", "/actuator/info").permitAll()
66:                         .requestMatchers("/api/passenger/**").hasAnyRole("PASSENGER", "DISPATCHER", "ADMIN")
67:                         .requestMatchers("/api/passenger/preferences/**").hasAnyRole("PASSENGER", "DISPATCHER", "ADMIN")
68:                         .requestMatchers("/api/dispatcher/**").hasAnyRole("DISPATCHER", "ADMIN")
69:                         .requestMatchers("/api/admin/**").hasRole("ADMIN")
70:                         .anyRequest().authenticated())
71:                 .authenticationProvider(authenticationProvider())
72:                 .addFilterBefore(jwtAuthenticationFilter, UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.class);
73: 
74:         return http.build();
75:     }
76: 
77:     @Bean
78:     public AuthenticationManager authenticationManager(AuthenticationConfiguration configuration) throws Exception {
79:         return configuration.getAuthenticationManager();
80:     }
81: 
82:     @Bean
83:     public DaoAuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider() {
84:         DaoAuthenticationProvider authProvider = new DaoAuthenticationProvider();
85:         authProvider.setUserDetailsService(userDetailsService);
86:         authProvider.setPasswordEncoder(passwordEncoder());
87:         return authProvider;
88:     }
89: 
90:     @Bean
91:     public PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder() {
92:         return new BCryptPasswordEncoder();
93:     }
94: }

(End of file - total 94 lines)
</content>

25:

28:         row1.setStopName("Beijing Central");
29:         row1.setKeywords("central,hub,main station");
30:         row1.setPinyin("beijing");
31:         row1.setInitials("bj");
32:         row1.setFrequencyPriority(95);
33:         row1.setStopPopularity(980);
34:

36:         row2.setRouteNumber("BJ7");
37:         row2.setStopName("Beijing Central");
38:         row2.setKeywords("beijing,north,gate");
39:         row2.setPinyin("beijing");
40:         row2.setInitials("bj");
41:         row2.setFrequencyPriority(85);

43: 
44:         when(transitStopRepository.findAll()).thenReturn(List.of(row1, row2));
45:         service = new SearchService(configRepository, transitStopRepository, new SimpleMeterRegistry(), observabilityService);
46:     }
47: 
48:     @Test
49:     void supportsInitialLetterMatching() {
50:         SearchResponse response = service.search("bj");

54: 
55:     @Test

60:                 .count();

63: 
64:     @Test

66:         SearchResponse response = service.search("beijing");

69: }

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.messagecenter;
2: 
3: import jakarta.validation.Valid;
4: import jakarta.validation.constraints.Positive;
5: import org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;
6: import org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;
7: import org.springframework.security.access.prepost.PreAuthorize;
8: import org.springframework.security.core.Authentication;
9: import org.springframework.validation.annotation.Validated;
10: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.DeleteMapping;
11: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;
12: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PathVariable;
13: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PostMapping;
14: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PutMapping;
15: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestBody;
16: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;
17: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestParam;
18: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;
19: 
20: import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
21: import java.security.MessageDigest;
22: import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;
23: import java.util.List;
24: import java.util.Map;
25: 
26: @RestController
27: @RequestMapping("/api/passenger/messages-center")
28: @Validated
29: public class MessageCenterController {
30: 
31:     private final MessageCenterService messageCenterService;
32:     private final BookingEventRepository bookingRepository;
33: 
34:     public MessageCenterController(MessageCenterService messageCenterService,
35:                                    BookingEventRepository bookingRepository) {
36:         this.messageCenterService = messageCenterService;
37:         this.bookingRepository = bookingRepository;
38:     }
39: 
40:     @GetMapping
41:     @PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole('PASSENGER', 'DISPATCHER', 'ADMIN')")
42:     public ResponseEntity<List<MessageResponse>> list(
43:             @RequestParam(name = "type", required = false) MessageType type,
44:             Authentication authentication) {
45:         return ResponseEntity.ok(messageCenterService.listMessages(authentication.getName(), type));
46:     }
47: 
48:     @PutMapping("/{id}/read")
49:     @PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole('PASSENGER', 'DISPATCHER', 'ADMIN')")
50:     public ResponseEntity<MessageResponse> markRead(
51:             @PathVariable @Positive Long id,
52:             @Valid @RequestBody MessageReadRequest request,
53:             Authentication authentication) {
54:         return ResponseEntity.ok(messageCenterService.markRead(authentication.getName(), id, request.read()));
55:     }
56: 
57:     @DeleteMapping("/{id}")
58:     @PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole('PASSENGER', 'DISPATCHER', 'ADMIN')")
59:     public ResponseEntity<Void> delete(
60:             @PathVariable @Positive Long id,
61:             Authentication authentication) {
62:         messageCenterService.delete(authentication.getName(), id);
63:         return ResponseEntity.noContent().build();
64:     }
65: 
66:     @PostMapping("/booking-events")
67:     @PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole('PASSENGER', 'DISPATCHER', 'ADMIN')")
68:     public ResponseEntity<Map<String, Long>> createBooking(
69:             @Valid @RequestBody BookingEventRequest request,
70:             Authentication authentication) {
71:         BookingEventEntity event = new BookingEventEntity();
72:         event.setUsername(authentication.getName());
73:         event.setRouteNumber(request.routeNumber());
74:         event.setPassengerPhoneToken(hashToken(request.passengerPhone()));
75:         event.setPassengerIdCardToken(hashToken(request.passengerIdCard()));
76:         event.setStartTime(request.startTime());
77:         event.setReservationSuccessSent(false);
78:         event.setArrivalReminderSent(false);
79:         event.setMissedCheckInSent(false);
80:         BookingEventEntity saved = bookingRepository.save(event);
81:         return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.CREATED).body(Map.of("bookingEventId", saved.getId()));
82:     }
83: 
84:     private String hashToken(String value) {
85:         try {
86:             MessageDigest digest = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256");
87:             byte[] out = digest.digest(value.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8));
88:             StringBuilder hex = new StringBuilder();
89:             for (byte b : out) {
90:                 hex.append(String.format("%02x", b));
91:             }
92:             return hex.toString();
93:         } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException ex) {
94:             throw new IllegalStateException("SHA-256 unavailable", ex);
95:         }
96:     }
97: }

(End of file - total 97 lines)

<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.messagecenter;
2: 
3: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
4: 
5: import java.util.regex.Pattern;
6: 
7: @Service
8: public class MessageMaskingService {
9: 
10:     private static final Pattern PHONE_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("(\\b\\d{3})\\d{4}(\\d{4}\\b)");
11:     private static final Pattern ID_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("(\\b[A-Za-z0-9]{3})[A-Za-z0-9]{8,12}([A-Za-z0-9]{3}\\b)");
12: 
13:     public String mask(String value) {
14:         if (value == null || value.isBlank()) {
15:             return value;
16:         }
17:         String maskedPhone = PHONE_PATTERN.matcher(value).replaceAll("$1****$2");
18:         return ID_PATTERN.matcher(maskedPhone).replaceAll("$1********$2");
19:     }
20: }

<content>1: #!/bin/bash
2: set -euo pipefail
3: 
4: ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
5: BASE_URL="${BASE_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"
6: 
7: if [ -f "${ROOT_DIR}/.env" ]; then
8:   set -a
9:   source "${ROOT_DIR}/.env"
10:   set +a
11: fi
12: 
13: PASS=0
14: FAIL=0
15: TOTAL=0
16: ERROR_LOG="${ROOT_DIR}/API_tests/error.log"
17: > "$ERROR_LOG"
18: 
19: require_cmd() {
20:   if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
21:     echo "Missing required command: $1" | tee -a "$ERROR_LOG"
22:     exit 1
23:   fi
24: }
25: 
26: require_cmd curl
27: 
28: JSON_TOOL=""
29: if command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
30:   JSON_TOOL="jq"
31: elif command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
32:   JSON_TOOL="python3"
33: else
34:   echo "Missing required JSON tool: install jq or python3" | tee -a "$ERROR_LOG"
35:   exit 1
36: fi
37: 
38: CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT="${CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT:-3}"
39: CURL_MAX_TIME="${CURL_MAX_TIME:-12}"
40: 
41: CURL_BASE_OPTS=(
42:   --silent
43:   --show-error
44:   --connect-timeout "$CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT"
45:   --max-time "$CURL_MAX_TIME"
46: )
47: 
48: json_extract_token() {
49:   local body="$1"
50:   if [ "$JSON_TOOL" = "jq" ]; then
51:     echo "$body" | jq -r '.token // empty' 2>/dev/null || true
52:   else
53:     python3 - <<'PY' "$body"
54: import json,sys
55: try:
56:     print(json.loads(sys.argv[1]).get("token", ""))
57: except Exception:
58:     print("")
59: PY
60:   fi
61: }
62: 
63: json_array_len() {
64:   local body="$1"
65:   if [ "$JSON_TOOL" = "jq" ]; then
66:     echo "$body" | jq 'length' 2>/dev/null || echo 0
67:   else
68:     python3 - <<'PY' "$body"
69: import json,sys
70: try:
71:     print(len(json.loads(sys.argv[1])))
72: except Exception:
73:     print(0)
74: PY
75:   fi
76: }
77: 
78: build_json_object() {
79:   local username="$1"
80:   local password="$2"
81:   if [ "$JSON_TOOL" = "jq" ]; then
82:     jq -cn --arg username "$username" --arg password "$password" '{username: $username, password: $password}'
83:   else
84:     python3 - <<'PY' "$username" "$password"
85: import json,sys
86: print(json.dumps({"username":sys.argv[1],"password":sys.argv[2]}))
87: PY
88:   fi
89: }
90: 
91: build_booking_payload() {
92:   local routeNumber="$1"
93:   local passengerPhone="$2"
94:   local passengerIdCard="$3"
95:   local startTime="$4"
96:   if [ "$JSON_TOOL" = "jq" ]; then
97:     jq -cn --arg routeNumber "$routeNumber" --arg passengerPhone "$passengerPhone" --arg passengerIdCard "$passengerIdCard" --arg startTime "$startTime" '{routeNumber: $routeNumber, passengerPhone: $passengerPhone, passengerIdCard: $passengerIdCard, startTime: $startTime}'
98:   else
99:     python3 - <<'PY' "$routeNumber" "$passengerPhone" "$passengerIdCard" "$startTime"
100: import json,sys
101: print(json.dumps({
102:   "routeNumber":sys.argv[1],
103:   "passengerPhone":sys.argv[2],
104:   "passengerIdCard":sys.argv[3],
105:   "startTime":sys.argv[4],
106: }))
107: PY
108:   fi
109: }
110: 
111: record() {
112:   local name="$1" ok="$2" details="${3:-}"
113:   TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
114:   if [ "$ok" = "1" ]; then
115:     PASS=$((PASS + 1))
116:     echo "[API] PASS: $name"
117:   else
118:     FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
119:     echo "[API] FAIL: $name" | tee -a "$ERROR_LOG"
120:     if [ -n "$details" ]; then
121:       echo "  $details" | tee -a "$ERROR_LOG"
122:     fi
123:   fi
124: }
125: 
126: request() {
127:   local method="$1" url="$2" token="${3:-}" body="${4:-}"
128:   local headers=(-H "Content-Type: application/json")
129:   local response=""
130:   local rc=0
131:   if [ -n "$token" ]; then
132:     headers+=(-H "Authorization: Bearer ${token}")
133:   fi
134:   set +e
135:   if [ -n "$body" ]; then
136:     response=$(curl "${CURL_BASE_OPTS[@]}" -X "$method" "${url}" "${headers[@]}" -d "$body")
137:   else
138:     response=$(curl "${CURL_BASE_OPTS[@]}" -X "$method" "${url}" "${headers[@]}")
139:   fi
140:   rc=$?
141:   set -e
142:   if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
143:     echo ""
144:     return 0
145:   fi
146:   echo "$response"
147: }
148: 
149: request_status() {
150:   local method="$1" url="$2" token="${3:-}" body="${4:-}"
151:   local headers=(-H "Content-Type: application/json")
152:   local status_code="000"
153:   local rc=0
154:   if [ -n "$token" ]; then
155:     headers+=(-H "Authorization: Bearer ${token}")
156:   fi
157:   set +e
158:   if [ -n "$body" ]; then
159:     status_code=$(curl "${CURL_BASE_OPTS[@]}" -o /tmp/api_body.$$ -w "%{http_code}" -X "$method" "${url}" "${headers[@]}" -d "$body")
160:   else
161:     status_code=$(curl "${CURL_BASE_OPTS[@]}" -o /tmp/api_body.$$ -w "%{http_code}" -X "$method" "${url}" "${headers[@]}")
162:   fi
163:   rc=$?
164:   set -e
165:   if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
166:     echo "000"
167:     return 0
168:   fi
169:   echo "$status_code"
170: }
171: 
172: health_code=$(curl "${CURL_BASE_OPTS[@]}" -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${BASE_URL}/actuator/health" || true)
173: if [ "$health_code" != "200" ]; then
174:   echo "Backend not ready at ${BASE_URL} (health=${health_code})" | tee -a "$ERROR_LOG"
175:   echo "API_SUMMARY total=1 pass=0 fail=1"
176:   exit 1
177: fi
178: 
179: reset_stack_and_wait() {
180:   if ! command -v docker-compose >/dev/null 2>&1; then
181:     return 1
182:   fi
183: 
184:   echo "[API] Resetting docker stack for deterministic auth..." | tee -a "$ERROR_LOG"
185:   bash -lc "cd \"${ROOT_DIR}\" && docker-compose --env-file .env down -v --remove-orphans || true"
186:   bash -lc "cd \"${ROOT_DIR}\" && docker-compose --env-file .env up -d --build"
187: 
188:   local health=""
189:   for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
190:     health=$(curl "${CURL_BASE_OPTS[@]}" -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${BASE_URL}/actuator/health" || true)
191:     if [ "$health" = "200" ]; then
192:       return 0
193:     fi
194:     sleep 2
195:   done
196:   echo "[API] Backend failed health check after stack reset" | tee -a "$ERROR_LOG"
197:   return 1
198: }
199: 
200: ADMIN_USERNAME="${BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME:-admin}"
201: ADMIN_PASSWORD="${BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD:-}"
202: if [ -z "$ADMIN_PASSWORD" ]; then
203:   echo "BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD is required for API tests" | tee -a "$ERROR_LOG"
204:   echo "API_SUMMARY total=1 pass=0 fail=1"
205:   exit 1
206: fi
207: 
208: admin_login=$(build_json_object "$ADMIN_USERNAME" "$ADMIN_PASSWORD")
209: admin_body=$(request POST "${BASE_URL}/api/auth/login" "" "$admin_login")
210: admin_token=$(json_extract_token "$admin_body")
211: if [ -n "$admin_token" ]; then
212:   record "admin login" 1
213: else
214:   if [ "${API_AUTO_RESET_ON_AUTH_FAIL:-true}" = "true" ] && reset_stack_and_wait; then
215:     admin_body=$(request POST "${BASE_URL}/api/auth/login" "" "$admin_login")
216:     admin_token=$(json_extract_token "$admin_body")
217:   fi
218: 
219:   if [ -n "$admin_token" ]; then
220:     record "admin login" 1 "recovered after stack reset"
221:   else
222:     record "admin login" 0 "body=${admin_body}"
223:     echo "API_SUMMARY total=${TOTAL} pass=${PASS} fail=${FAIL}"
224:     echo "Failure logs: ${ERROR_LOG}"
225:     exit 1
226:   fi
227: fi
228: 
229: bad_login_code=$(request_status POST "${BASE_URL}/api/auth/login" "" '{"username":"admin","password":"bad"}')
230: if [ "$bad_login_code" = "401" ] || [ "$bad_login_code" = "400" ]; then
231:   record "invalid login rejected" 1
232: else
233:   record "invalid login rejected" 0 "status=${bad_login_code}"
234: fi
235: 
236: unauth_code=$(request_status GET "${BASE_URL}/api/admin/observability/snapshot" "")
237: if [ "$unauth_code" = "401" ] || [ "$unauth_code" = "403" ]; then
238:   record "unauthorized access blocked" 1
239: else
240:   record "unauthorized access blocked" 0 "status=${unauth_code}"
241: fi
242: 
243: create_user_body='{"username":"apitest_passenger","password":"pass12345","role":"PASSENGER"}'
244: create_user_code=$(request_status POST "${BASE_URL}/api/admin/users" "$admin_token" "$create_user_body")
245: if [ "$create_user_code" = "201" ] || [ "$create_user_code" = "400" ] || [ "$create_user_code" = "409" ]; then
246:   record "create passenger user" 1
247: else
248:   record "create passenger user" 0 "status=${create_user_code}"
249: fi
250: 
251: pass_login=$(request POST "${BASE_URL}/api/auth/login" "" '{"username":"apitest_passenger","password":"pass12345"}')
252: pass_token=$(json_extract_token "$pass_login")
253: if [ -n "$pass_token" ]; then
254:   record "passenger login" 1
255: else
256:   record "passenger login" 0 "body=${pass_login}"
257: fi
258: 
259: before_count=$(json_array_len "$(request GET "${BASE_URL}/api/passenger/messages-center" "$pass_token")")
260: 
261: start_time=$(date -u -d '-6 minutes' '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ' 2>/dev/null || date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ')
262: 
263: booking_payload=$(build_booking_payload "A1" "13812345678" "ABC123456789XYZ" "$start_time")
264: 
265: booking_code=$(request_status POST "${BASE_URL}/api/passenger/messages-center/booking-events" "$pass_token" "$booking_payload")
266: if [ "$booking_code" = "201" ]; then
267:   record "booking event seed" 1
268: else
269:   record "booking event seed" 0 "status=${booking_code}"
270: fi
271: 
272: sleep 3

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    role VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (role IN ('PASSENGER', 'DISPATCHER', 'ADMIN')),
    enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    arrival_reminder_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    reservation_success_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    reminder_lead_minutes INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 10,
    dnd_start TIME,
    dnd_end TIME,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workflow_tasks (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    type VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('ROUTE_DATA_CHANGE', 'REMINDER_CONFIGURATION', 'ABNORMAL_DATA_REVIEW')),
    mode VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (mode IN ('CONDITIONAL', 'JOINT', 'PARALLEL')),
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('SUBMITTED', 'IN_REVIEW', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED', 'RETURNED')),
    title VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    payload VARCHAR(3000) NOT NULL,
    submitted_by VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    assigned_to VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    collaborators VARCHAR(1000) NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    current_step INTEGER NOT NULL,
    total_steps INTEGER NOT NULL,
    required_approvals INTEGER NOT NULL,
    received_approvals INTEGER NOT NULL,
    escalated BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    last_action_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS messages (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    type VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('RESERVATION_SUCCESS', 'ARRIVAL_REMINDER', 'MISSED_CHECK_IN')),
    title VARCHAR(160) NOT NULL,
    content VARCHAR(4000) NOT NULL,
    trace_id VARCHAR(80) NOT NULL,
    read_flag BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    masked BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS message_queue_events (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    type VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('RESERVATION_SUCCESS', 'ARRIVAL_REMINDER', 'MISSED_CHECK_IN')),
    title VARCHAR(160) NOT NULL,
    content VARCHAR(4000) NOT NULL,
    trace_id VARCHAR(80) NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('PENDING', 'PROCESSED')),
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS booking_events (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    route_number VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL,
    passenger_phone_token VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    passenger_id_card_token VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    start_time TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,
    reservation_success_sent BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    arrival_reminder_sent BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    missed_check_in_sent BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS import_jobs (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    format VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (format IN ('JSON', 'HTML')),
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('RUNNING', 'COMPLETED', 'FAILED')),
    source_name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    total_rows INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    success_rows INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    failed_rows INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS cleaned_records (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    import_job_id BIGINT NOT NULL,
    source_ref VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    stop_name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    address VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    apartment_type VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    area_standardized VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    price_standardized VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    raw_snapshot VARCHAR(4000) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    CONSTRAINT fk_cleaned_records_job FOREIGN KEY (import_job_id) REFERENCES import_jobs (id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS cleaning_audit_logs (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    import_job_id BIGINT NOT NULL,
    source_ref VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    field_name VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    raw_value VARCHAR(1000) NOT NULL,
    cleaned_value VARCHAR(1000) NOT NULL,
    action VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    CONSTRAINT fk_cleaning_audit_job FOREIGN KEY (import_job_id) REFERENCES import_jobs (id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stop_structure_versions (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    stop_name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    field_name VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    old_value VARCHAR(1000) NOT NULL,
    new_value VARCHAR(1000) NOT NULL,
    version_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
    changed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    import_job_id BIGINT NOT NULL,
    CONSTRAINT fk_stop_versions_job FOREIGN KEY (import_job_id) REFERENCES import_jobs (id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transit_stops (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    route_number VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL,
    stop_name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    keywords VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    pinyin VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    initials VARCHAR(80) NOT NULL,
    frequency_priority INTEGER NOT NULL,
    stop_popularity INTEGER NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS system_configs (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    config_key VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    config_value VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification_templates (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    template_key VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    subject VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    body VARCHAR(3000) NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS field_dictionaries (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    category VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    code VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    item_value VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS alert_diagnostics (

);

<content>1: #!/bin/bash
2: set -euo pipefail
3: 
4: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
5: ROOT_DIR="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
6: BACKEND_DIR="${ROOT_DIR}/backend"
7: ERROR_LOG="${ROOT_DIR}/unit_tests/error.log"
8: > "$ERROR_LOG"
9: 
10: TEST_CLASSES=(
11:   "AuthControllerTest"
12:   "MessagePrivacyServiceTest"
13:   "MessageCenterAuthorizationTest"
14:   "SearchServiceTest"
15:   "NotificationRuleServiceTest"
16:   "NotificationSettingsControllerTest"
17:   "RbacAccessTest"
18:   "ObservabilityServiceTest"
19:   "WorkflowAuthorizationTest"
20:   "WorkflowEscalationTest"
21:   "DataImportServiceTest"
22:   "ApiSecuritySemanticsTest"
23: )
24: 
25: TOTAL=${#TEST_CLASSES[@]}
26: PASS=0
27: FAIL=0
28: 
29: TEST_ARG=$(IFS=,; echo "${TEST_CLASSES[*]}")
30: 
31: run_maven_local() {
32:   bash -lc "cd \"${BACKEND_DIR}\" && mvn -q -Dtest=${TEST_ARG} test"
33: }
34: 
35: run_maven_docker() {
36:   local m2_volume="mindflow-m2-cache"
37:   bash -lc "MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1 docker run --rm -v \"${BACKEND_DIR}:/app\" -v \"${m2_volume}:/root/.m2\" -w \"/app\" maven:3.9.8-eclipse-temurin-17-alpine mvn -q -Dtest=${TEST_ARG} test"
38: }
39: 
40: echo "[UNIT] Running ${TOTAL} tests in one Maven invocation"
41: echo "[UNIT] Targets: ${TEST_ARG}"
42: 
43: if command -v mvn >/dev/null 2>&1; then
44:   if run_maven_local; then
45:     PASS=$TOTAL
46:   else
47:     FAIL=$TOTAL
48:     echo "[UNIT][FAIL] Maven local run failed" >> "$ERROR_LOG"
49:   fi
50: elif command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
51:   if run_maven_docker; then
52:     PASS=$TOTAL
53:   else
54:     FAIL=$TOTAL
55:     echo "[UNIT][FAIL] Maven docker run failed" >> "$ERROR_LOG"
56:   fi
57: else
58:   FAIL=$TOTAL
59:   echo "[UNIT] neither mvn nor docker found in environment" | tee -a "$ERROR_LOG"
60: fi
61: 
62: echo "UNIT_SUMMARY total=${TOTAL} pass=${PASS} fail=${FAIL}"
63: if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
64:   echo "Failure logs: ${ERROR_LOG}"
65:   exit 1
66: fi

(End of file - total 66 lines)
</content>

The generated files exist under `/Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/frontend/dist/mindflow-auth-frontend`.

The backend entry and tests are statically confirmable from `backend/Dockerfile:1-20`, `backend/pom.xml:1-79`, `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:27-33`, and `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh:172-284`.

13: 
14: ## Prioritized Issues
15: 
16: ### High
17: 
18: 1. Imported/admin-managed data is not connected to passenger search, so the search experience runs on seeded sample data instead of the integration pipeline output.
19:   - Evidence: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/search/SearchService.java:167-170`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/search/TransitStopBootstrapService.java:18-33`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/integration/DataImportService.java:165-179`
20:   - Impact: Core business data changes made by admins/import jobs do not propagate to the passenger-facing search feature, weakening the prompt's "bus data integration + version management + search" story.
21:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Persist validated import output into `transit_stops` or a canonical stop catalog and rebuild search from that canonical source instead of the bootstrap sample set.
22: 2. Workflow timeout escalation warnings are manual, not automatic after 24 hours as required.
23:   - Evidence: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowController.java:85-89`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowService.java:107-120`, `README.md:63-64`
24:   - Impact: The delivered workflow engine does not autonomously raise timeout alerts; operators only get escalation if someone manually triggers the evaluation endpoint or clicks the UI button.
25:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add a scheduled job that evaluates stale workflow tasks and writes an alert/diagnostic record without manual intervention.
26: 
27: ### Medium
28: 
29: 1. Notification templates and field dictionaries are CRUD-only; runtime flows do not consume them.
30:   - Evidence: template CRUD in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/admin/AdminControlService.java:67-95`; hardcoded notification message bodies in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:37-45`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:66-71`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:83-87`; hardcoded import field mapping in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/integration/DataImportService.java:153-157`; dictionary CRUD only in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/admin/AdminControlService.java:98-129`
31:   - Impact: Admin edits do not materially change notification generation or field mapping behavior, so the "maintain templates / dictionaries" capability is only partially operational.
32:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Resolve notification titles/bodies from `notification_templates` and use `field_dictionaries` during import field normalization/mapping.
33: 2. Sensitivity-level desensitization is not integrated into the real message-center pipeline.
34:   - Evidence: real inbox records have no sensitivity field in `backend/src/main/resources/schema.sql:36-57`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageEntity.java:23-46`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageQueueEventEntity.java:23-44`; queue masking is generic regex masking in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageQueueService.java:35-44`; sensitivity logic exists only in the separate demo endpoint/service at `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/message/MessageController.java:24-37` and `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/message/MessagePrivacyService.java:9-27`
35:   - Impact: The prompt requires desensitization "based on sensitivity levels," but the actual inbox path does not carry or enforce sensitivity levels.
36:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add sensitivity metadata to queued/inbox messages and apply `MessagePrivacyService` during message delivery/rendering.
37: 3. Client-side route authorization is absent; browser users can navigate directly to admin/dispatcher pages and rely solely on backend rejection.
38:   - Evidence: `frontend/src/app/app.routes.ts:10-18`, `frontend/src/main.ts:9-14`, no route guard definitions found under `frontend/src/app/`
39:   - Impact: This is not a server-side privilege-escalation bug because backend RBAC remains enforced, but it is still a feature-level authorization weakness and a weaker real-product experience.
40:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add route guards for authentication and role-aware navigation hiding.
41: 4. Test coverage is not sufficient to catch the vast majority of core defects.
42:   - Evidence: no frontend specs under `fullstack/frontend/src`; backend tests listed in `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:10-19` and `backend/src/test/java/**`; `AuthControllerTest` exists at `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/AuthControllerTest.java:1-79` but is omitted from the default unit runner in `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:10-19`; API coverage is narrow in `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh:229-278`
43:   - Impact: Major flows can regress while the provided test entry points still appear acceptable.
44:   - Minimal improvement suggestion: Expand automated tests for message-center ownership, workflow escalation, import HTML/versioning, admin-template application, search edge cases, and frontend component flows.
45: 
46: ### Low
47: 
48: 1. README startup instructions are present but incomplete because they do not explicitly tell the operator to create `.env` from `.env.example` before Docker Compose startup.
49:   - Evidence: `README.md:9-29`, required environment values in `.env.example:1-17`, backend startup validation in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/config/SecurityStartupValidator.java:37-75`
50:   - Impact: First-time operators can hit backend startup failures or blank compose variables even though the repo already provides the example file.

372: ### Overall Judgment on Whether the Tests Are Sufficient to Catch the Vast Majority of Problems
373: 
374: - Conclusion: `Fail`
375: - Reason (Judgment Boundary): The current tests do cover some important foundations, especially RBAC basics, workflow ownership, search ranking/dedup happy paths, DND logic, JSON-cleaning happy path, and selected API semantics. However, they do not cover enough of the highest-risk business paths to catch the vast majority of serious defects. Missing areas include message-center ownership, notification preference controller validation, workflow mode semantics and automatic escalation, import HTML/audit/versioning, admin runtime template/dictionary usage, observability alert triggering, frontend behavior, and multiple edge/boundary cases. Because of those gaps, the project could still have severe defects while the provided test suite appears healthy.
376: - Minimal improvement suggestions by priority:
377:   1. Add ownership tests for message-center read/delete and add automatic workflow-escalation tests.
378:   2. Add integration tests for notification preferences, search API, admin template application, and import HTML/audit/versioning.
379:   3. Add observability alert-trigger tests and a small frontend test suite for login, route gating, and core screens.
380:   4. Add boundary tests for empty results, time windows, duplicate actions, and malformed inputs.

382: ## Final Acceptance Summary
383: 
384: - The project is substantial and clearly more than a demo. It contains a real full-stack structure, Angular UI, Spring Boot backend, PostgreSQL schema, queueing, admin screens, workflow features, and observability.
385: - The acceptance result is not a full pass because several prompt-critical behaviors are only partially realized:
386:   - imported/admin-managed data does not power the passenger search experience
387:   - workflow timeout warnings are manual instead of automatic
388:   - admin-maintained templates/dictionaries do not drive runtime behavior
389:   - sensitivity-level masking is separated from the real inbox pipeline
390:   - test coverage is not broad enough for the main risk areas
391: - Acceptance verdict: `Partially Pass`
392: 

(End of file - total 392 lines)
</content>

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ROOT_DIR="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
BACKEND_DIR="${ROOT_DIR}/backend"
ERROR_LOG="${ROOT_DIR}/unit_tests/error.log"
> "$ERROR_LOG"

TEST_CLASSES=(
  "AuthControllerTest"
  "MessagePrivacyServiceTest"
  "MessageCenterAuthorizationTest"
  "SearchServiceTest"
  "NotificationRuleServiceTest"
  "NotificationSettingsControllerTest"
  "RbacAccessTest"
  "ObservabilityServiceTest"
  "WorkflowAuthorizationTest"
  "WorkflowEscalationTest"
  "DataImportServiceTest"
  "ApiSecuritySemanticsTest"
)

TOTAL=${#TEST_CLASSES[@]}
PASS=0
FAIL=0

TEST_ARG=$(IFS=,; echo "${TEST_CLASSES[*]}")

run_maven_local() {
  bash -lc "cd \"${BACKEND_DIR}\" && mvn -q -Dtest=${TEST_ARG} test"
}

run_maven_docker() {
  local m2_volume="mindflow-m2-cache"
  bash -lc "MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1 docker run --rm -v \"${BACKEND_DIR}:/app\" -v \"${m2_volume}:/root/.m2\" -w \"/app\" maven:3.9.8-eclipse-temurin-17-alpine mvn -q -Dtest=${TEST_ARG} test"
}

echo "[UNIT] Running ${TOTAL} tests in one Maven invocation"
echo "[UNIT] Targets: ${TEST_ARG}"

if command -v mvn >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if run_maven_local; then
    PASS=$TOTAL
  else
    FAIL=$TOTAL
    echo "[UNIT][FAIL] Maven local run failed" >> "$ERROR_LOG"
  fi
elif command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if run_maven_docker; then
    PASS=$TOTAL
  else
    FAIL=$TOTAL
    echo "[UNIT][FAIL] Maven docker run failed" >> "$ERROR_LOG"
  fi
else
  FAIL=$TOTAL
  echo "[UNIT] neither mvn nor docker found in environment" | tee -a "$ERROR_LOG"
fi

echo "UNIT_SUMMARY total=${TOTAL} pass=${PASS} fail=${FAIL}"
if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Failure logs: ${ERROR_LOG}"
  exit 1
fi

61:     @Transactional

81:                     job.setSuccessRows(job.getSuccessRows() + 1);

118:         });

136:                 continue;
137:             }
138:             List<Element> cols = row.select("td");
139:             if (cols.size() < 5) {
140:                 continue;
141:             }
142:             Map<String, String> fields = Map.of(
143:                     "stop", cols.get(0).text(),
144:                     "address", cols.get(1).text(),
145:                     "apartment", cols.get(2).text(),
146:                     "area", cols.get(3).text(),
147:                     "price", cols.get(4).text()
148:             );
149:             parsed.add(new RawImportRecord("html-row-" + i, fields, row.outerHtml()));
150:             i++;
151:         }
152:         return parsed;
153:     }
154: 
155:     private void cleanAndPersist(Long jobId, RawImportRecord raw, List<String> notes) {
156:         Map<String, String> map = raw.fields();
157: 
158:         String stop = mapped(map, "stop", "stopName", "station_name");
159:         String address = mapped(map, "address", "addr", "location");
160:         String apartment = mapped(map, "apartment", "aptType", "house_type");
161:         String area = mapped(map, "area", "area_size", "size");
162:         String price = mapped(map, "price", "rent", "monthly_price");
163: 
164:         String cleanStop = normalizeOrNull("stop_name", stop, raw.sourceRef(), jobId, notes);
165:         String cleanAddress = normalizeOrNull("address", applyDictionary("address", address), raw.sourceRef(), jobId, notes);
166:         String cleanApartment = normalizeOrNull("apartment_type", applyDictionary("apartment_type", apartment), raw.sourceRef(), jobId, notes);
167:         String cleanArea = normalizeArea(area, raw.sourceRef(), jobId, notes);
168:         String cleanPrice = normalizePrice(price, raw.sourceRef(), jobId, notes);
169: 
170:         CleanedRecordEntity entity = new CleanedRecordEntity();
171:         entity.setImportJobId(jobId);
172:         entity.setSourceRef(raw.sourceRef());
173:         entity.setStopName(cleanStop);
174:         entity.setAddress(cleanAddress);
175:         entity.setApartmentType(cleanApartment);
176:         entity.setAreaStandardized(cleanArea);
177:         entity.setPriceStandardized(cleanPrice);
178:         entity.setRawSnapshot(raw.rawSnapshot());
179:         cleanedRecordRepository.save(entity);
180: 
181:         versionFieldIfChanged(cleanStop, "address", cleanAddress, jobId);
182:         versionFieldIfChanged(cleanStop, "apartment_type", cleanApartment, jobId);
183:         versionFieldIfChanged(cleanStop, "area_standardized", cleanArea, jobId);
184:         versionFieldIfChanged(cleanStop, "price_standardized", cleanPrice, jobId);
185: 
186:         upsertTransitStop(cleanStop, cleanAddress, cleanApartment);
187:     }
188: 
189:     private void upsertTransitStop(String stopName, String address, String apartmentType) {
190:         if (stopName == null || stopName.isBlank() || "NULL".equalsIgnoreCase(stopName)) {
191:             return;
192:         }
193:         String normalizedStop = stopName.trim();
194: 
195:         TransitStopEntity stop = transitStopRepository.findTopByStopNameIgnoreCase(normalizedStop)
196:                 .orElseGet(TransitStopEntity::new);
197: 
198:         if (stop.getRouteNumber() == null || stop.getRouteNumber().isBlank()) {
199:             stop.setRouteNumber("IMP-" + Math.abs(normalizedStop.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT).hashCode() % 1000));
200:         }
201:         stop.setStopName(normalizedStop);
202:         stop.setPinyin(toAsciiWords(normalizedStop));
203:         stop.setInitials(toInitials(normalizedStop));
204:         stop.setKeywords(buildKeywords(address, apartmentType, normalizedStop));
205:         if (stop.getFrequencyPriority() <= 0) {
206:             stop.setFrequencyPriority(50);
207:         }
208:         if (stop.getStopPopularity() <= 0) {
209:             stop.setStopPopularity(300);
210:         }
211:         transitStopRepository.save(stop);
212:     }
213: 
214:     private String buildKeywords(String address, String apartmentType, String stopName) {
215:         String mappedApartment = applyDictionary("apartment_type", apartmentType);
216:         String mappedAddress = applyDictionary("address", address);
217:         return String.join(",",
218:                 safeKeyword(stopName),
219:                 safeKeyword(mappedAddress),
220:                 safeKeyword(mappedApartment));

223:     private String applyDictionary(String category, String rawValue) {
224:         if (rawValue == null || rawValue.isBlank()) {
225:             return rawValue;
226:         }
227:         String trimmed = rawValue.trim();
228:         return adminControlService.resolveDictionaryValue(category, trimmed)
229:                 .orElse(trimmed);
230:     }
231: 
232:     private String safeKeyword(String value) {
233:         if (value == null || value.isBlank()) {
234:             return "unknown";
235:         }
236:         return value.trim().toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT).replaceAll("\\s+", " ");
237:     }
238: 
239:     private String toAsciiWords(String value) {
240:         if (value == null || value.isBlank()) {
241:             return "stop";
242:         }
243:         String normalized = value.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT).replaceAll("[^a-z0-9 ]", " ").replaceAll("\\s+", " ").trim();
244:         return normalized.isBlank() ? "stop" : normalized;
245:     }
246: 
247:     private String toInitials(String value) {
248:         if (value == null || value.isBlank()) {
249:             return "s";
250:         }
251:         String[] parts = toAsciiWords(value).split(" ");
252:         StringBuilder initials = new StringBuilder();
253:         for (String part : parts) {
254:             if (!part.isBlank()) {
255:                 initials.append(part.charAt(0));
256:             }
257:         }
258:         return initials.isEmpty() ? "s" : initials.toString();
259:     }
