261:     private void versionFieldIfChanged(String stopName, String fieldName, String newValue, Long jobId) {
262:         Optional<StopStructureVersionEntity> latest = stopStructureVersionRepository
263:                 .findTopByStopNameAndFieldNameOrderByVersionNumberDesc(stopName, fieldName);
264:         String old = latest.map(StopStructureVersionEntity::getNewValue).orElse("NULL");
265:         if (old.equals(newValue)) {
266:             return;
267:         }
268:         StopStructureVersionEntity version = new StopStructureVersionEntity();
269:         version.setStopName(stopName);
270:         version.setFieldName(fieldName);
271:         version.setOldValue(old);
272:         version.setNewValue(newValue);
273:         version.setVersionNumber(latest.map(v -> v.getVersionNumber() + 1).orElse(1));
274:         version.setImportJobId(jobId);
275:         stopStructureVersionRepository.save(version);
276:     }
277: 
278:     private String mapped(Map<String, String> map, String... keys) {
279:         for (String key : keys) {
280:             String value = map.get(key);
281:             if (value != null && !value.isBlank()) {
282:                 return value;
283:             }
284:         }
285:         return null;
286:     }
287: 
288:     private String normalizeOrNull(String fieldName, String value, String sourceRef, Long jobId, List<String> notes) {
289:         String missingMarker = adminControlService.getCleaningRules().missingValueMarker();
290:         boolean trimEnabled = adminControlService.getCleaningRules().trimEnabled();
291:         if (value == null || value.isBlank()) {
292:             audit(jobId, sourceRef, fieldName, "", missingMarker, "MISSING_TO_NULL");
293:             notes.add("Missing value mapped to " + missingMarker + " for " + fieldName + " from " + sourceRef);
294:             return missingMarker;
295:         }
296:         String cleaned = trimEnabled ? value.trim() : value;
297:         if (!cleaned.equals(value)) {
298:             audit(jobId, sourceRef, fieldName, value, cleaned, "TRIMMED");
299:         }
300:         return cleaned;
301:     }
302: 
303:     private String normalizeArea(String value, String sourceRef, Long jobId, List<String> notes) {
304:         String areaUnit = adminControlService.getCleaningRules().areaUnit();
305:         String missingMarker = adminControlService.getCleaningRules().missingValueMarker();
306:         if (value == null || value.isBlank()) {
307:             audit(jobId, sourceRef, "area", "", missingMarker, "MISSING_TO_NULL");
308:             notes.add("Missing area mapped to " + missingMarker + " from " + sourceRef);
309:             return missingMarker;
310:         }
311:         String numeric = extractFirstNumber(value);
312:         String cleaned = numeric == null ? missingMarker : numeric + " " + areaUnit;
313:         audit(jobId, sourceRef, "area", value, cleaned, "UNIT_STANDARDIZED");
314:         return cleaned;
315:     }
316: 
317:     private String normalizePrice(String value, String sourceRef, Long jobId, List<String> notes) {
318:         String priceUnit = adminControlService.getCleaningRules().priceUnit();
319:         String missingMarker = adminControlService.getCleaningRules().missingValueMarker();
320:         if (value == null || value.isBlank()) {
321:             audit(jobId, sourceRef, "price", "", missingMarker, "MISSING_TO_NULL");
322:             notes.add("Missing price mapped to " + missingMarker + " from " + sourceRef);
323:             return missingMarker;
324:         }
325:         String numeric = extractFirstNumber(value);
326:         String cleaned = numeric == null ? missingMarker : numeric + " " + priceUnit;
327:         audit(jobId, sourceRef, "price", value, cleaned, "UNIT_STANDARDIZED");
328:         return cleaned;
329:     }
330: 
331:     private String extractFirstNumber(String value) {
332:         if (value == null) {
333:             return null;
334:         }
335:         Matcher matcher = FIRST_NUMBER.matcher(value.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT));
336:         if (!matcher.find()) {
337:             return null;
338:         }
339:         return matcher.group();
340:     }
341: 
342:     private void audit(Long jobId, String sourceRef, String fieldName, String raw, String cleaned, String action) {
343:         CleaningAuditEntity log = new CleaningAuditEntity();
344:         log.setImportJobId(jobId);

348:         log.setCleanedValue(cleaned == null ? "" : cleaned);
349:         log.setAction(action);
350:         cleaningAuditRepository.save(log);
351:     }

<content>1: package com.mindflow.security.workflow;
2: 
3: import com.mindflow.security.common.OwnershipDeniedException;
4: import com.mindflow.security.common.ResourceNotFoundException;
5: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
6: import org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;
7: 
8: import java.time.Duration;
9: import java.time.Instant;
10: import java.util.LinkedHashMap;
11: import java.util.List;
12: import java.util.Map;
13: import java.util.Set;
14: 
15: @Service
16: public class WorkflowService {
17: 
18:     private static final Duration ESCALATION_TIMEOUT = Duration.ofHours(24);
19: 
20:     private final WorkflowTaskRepository repository;
21:     private final WorkflowStateMachineService stateMachineService;
22: 
23:     public WorkflowService(WorkflowTaskRepository repository,
24:                            WorkflowStateMachineService stateMachineService) {
25:         this.repository = repository;
26:         this.stateMachineService = stateMachineService;
27:     }
28: 
29:     @Transactional
30:     public WorkflowTaskResponse createTask(CreateWorkflowTaskRequest request, String submittedBy) {
31:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = new WorkflowTaskEntity();
32:         task.setType(request.type());
33:         task.setMode(request.mode());
34:         task.setStatus(WorkflowStatus.SUBMITTED);
35:         task.setTitle(request.title());
36:         task.setPayload(request.payload());
37:         task.setSubmittedBy(submittedBy);
38:         task.setAssignedTo(submittedBy);
39:         Set<String> collaborators = task.collaboratorsAsSet();
40:         collaborators.add(submittedBy);
41:         if (request.collaborators() != null) {
42:             collaborators.addAll(request.collaborators());
43:         }
44:         task.setCollaboratorsFromSet(collaborators);
45:         task.setCurrentStep(1);
46:         task.setTotalSteps(3);
47:         int required = request.requiredApprovals() == null ? defaultApprovals(request.mode()) : Math.max(1, request.requiredApprovals());
48:         task.setRequiredApprovals(required);
49:         task.setReceivedApprovals(0);
50:         task.setEscalated(false);
51:         task.setLastActionAt(Instant.now());
52:         return toResponse(repository.save(task));
53:     }
54: 
55:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
56:     public List<WorkflowTaskResponse> listTasks(String actor) {
57:         Map<Long, WorkflowTaskResponse> merged = new LinkedHashMap<>();
58:         for (WorkflowTaskEntity task : repository.findByAssignedToOrderByCreatedAtDesc(actor)) {
59:             merged.put(task.getId(), toResponse(task));
60:         }
61:         for (WorkflowTaskEntity task : repository.findByCollaboratorsContainingOrderByCreatedAtDesc(actor)) {
62:             if (canAccess(task, actor)) {
63:                 merged.put(task.getId(), toResponse(task));
64:             }
65:         }
66:         return merged.values().stream().toList();
67:     }
68: 
69:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
70:     public WorkflowDetailsResponse getTask(Long id, String actor) {
71:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = fetch(id, actor);
72:         return new WorkflowDetailsResponse(toResponse(task), buildProgress(task));
73:     }
74: 
75:     @Transactional
76:     public WorkflowTaskResponse approve(Long id, String actor) {
77:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = fetch(id, actor);
78:         stateMachineService.approve(task);
79:         task.setLastActionAt(Instant.now());
80:         task.setEscalated(false);
81:         return toResponse(repository.save(task));
82:     }
83: 
84:     @Transactional
85:     public WorkflowTaskResponse reject(Long id, String actor) {
86:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = fetch(id, actor);
87:         stateMachineService.reject(task);
88:         task.setLastActionAt(Instant.now());
89:         task.setEscalated(false);
90:         return toResponse(repository.save(task));
91:     }
92: 
93:     @Transactional
94:     public WorkflowTaskResponse returnToSubmitter(Long id, String actor) {
95:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = fetch(id, actor);
96:         stateMachineService.returnToSubmitter(task);
97:         task.setLastActionAt(Instant.now());
98:         task.setEscalated(false);
99:         return toResponse(repository.save(task));
100:     }
101: 
102:     @Transactional
103:     public List<WorkflowTaskResponse> batchApprove(List<Long> ids, String actor) {
104:         return ids.stream().map(id -> approve(id, actor)).toList();
105:     }
106: 
107:     @Transactional
108:     public List<WorkflowTaskResponse> evaluateEscalations() {
109:         Instant cutoff = Instant.now().minus(ESCALATION_TIMEOUT);
110:         List<WorkflowTaskEntity> staleTasks = repository.findByStatusInAndLastActionAtBefore(
111:                 List.of(WorkflowStatus.SUBMITTED, WorkflowStatus.IN_REVIEW),
112:                 cutoff
113:         );
114: 
115:         for (WorkflowTaskEntity task : staleTasks) {
116:             task.setEscalated(true);
117:         }
118:         repository.saveAll(staleTasks);
119:         return staleTasks.stream().map(this::toResponse).toList();
120:     }
121: 
122:     private WorkflowTaskEntity fetch(Long id, String actor) {
123:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = repository.findById(id)
124:                 .orElseThrow(() -> new ResourceNotFoundException("Task not found"));
125:         if (!canAccess(task, actor)) {
126:             throw new OwnershipDeniedException("Workflow task access denied for this user");
127:         }
128:         return task;
129:     }
130: 
131:     private boolean canAccess(WorkflowTaskEntity task, String actor) {
132:         if (actor.equals(task.getAssignedTo())) {
133:             return true;
134:         }
135:         if (task.getMode() == WorkflowMode.CONDITIONAL) {
136:             return false;
137:         }
138:         return task.collaboratorsAsSet().contains(actor);
139:     }
140: 
141:     private int defaultApprovals(WorkflowMode mode) {
142:         return switch (mode) {
143:             case JOINT, PARALLEL -> 2;
144:             case CONDITIONAL -> 1;
145:         };
146:     }
147: 
148:     private List<WorkflowProgressStep> buildProgress(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
149:         return List.of(
150:                 new WorkflowProgressStep("Submitted", task.getCurrentStep() >= 1, task.getCurrentStep() == 1),
151:                 new WorkflowProgressStep("In Review", task.getCurrentStep() >= 2, task.getCurrentStep() == 2),
152:                 new WorkflowProgressStep("Final Decision", task.getCurrentStep() >= 3 || isFinal(task), task.getCurrentStep() == 3 || isFinal(task))
153:         );
154:     }
155: 
156:     private boolean isFinal(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
157:         return task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.APPROVED ||
158:                 task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.REJECTED ||
159:                 task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.RETURNED;
160:     }
161: 
162:     private WorkflowTaskResponse toResponse(WorkflowTaskEntity task) {
163:         boolean timeout = Duration.between(task.getLastActionAt(), Instant.now()).toHours() >= 24
164:                 && (task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.SUBMITTED || task.getStatus() == WorkflowStatus.IN_REVIEW);
165:         return new WorkflowTaskResponse(
166:                 task.getId(),
167:                 task.getType(),
168:                 task.getMode(),
169:                 task.getStatus(),
170:                 task.getTitle(),
171:                 task.getPayload(),
172:                 task.getSubmittedBy(),
173:                 task.getAssignedTo(),
174:                 task.getCurrentStep(),
175:                 task.getTotalSteps(),
176:                 task.getRequiredApprovals(),
177:                 task.getReceivedApprovals(),
178:                 task.isEscalated(),
179:                 timeout,
180:                 task.getLastActionAt(),
181:                 task.getCreatedAt()
182:         );
183:     }
184: }

39:     public void recordWorkflowLog(String workflow, String detail) {
40:         String trace = TraceContext.getTraceId();
41:         log.info("event=workflow activity={} traceId={} detail={}", workflow, trace, detail);
42:     }
43: 
44:     @Scheduled(fixedDelayString = "${app.monitoring.check-ms:30000}")
45:     @Transactional
46:     public void evaluateAlerts() {
47:         int backlog = queueEventRepository.findTop100ByStatusOrderByCreatedAtAsc(QueueStatus.PENDING).size();
48:         meterRegistry.gauge("app.queue.backlog", backlog);
49: 
50:         if (backlog > properties.getQueueBacklogThreshold()) {
51:             createAlert("QUEUE_BACKLOG", "WARN",
52:                     "Queue backlog is " + backlog + " which exceeds threshold " + properties.getQueueBacklogThreshold());
53:         }
54: 
55:         Timer timer = meterRegistry.find("app.api.request.duration").timer();
56:         if (timer != null) {
57:             long p95Ms = Math.round(extractPercentileMs(timer, 0.95));
58:             if (p95Ms > MAX_REASONABLE_P95_MS) {
59:                 log.warn("event=alert-skip type=API_P95 reason=unrealistic_value valueMs={}", p95Ms);
60:                 return;
61:             }
62:             if (p95Ms > properties.getApiP95ThresholdMs()) {
63:                 createAlert("API_P95", "WARN",
64:                         "API p95 latency is " + p95Ms + "ms which exceeds threshold " + properties.getApiP95ThresholdMs() + "ms");
65:             }
66:         }
67:     }
68: 
69:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
70:     public List<AlertDiagnosticEntity> recentDiagnostics() {
71:         return alertRepository.findTop100ByOrderByCreatedAtDesc();
72:     }
73: 
74:     private void createAlert(String type, String severity, String message) {
75:         AlertDiagnosticEntity alert = new AlertDiagnosticEntity();
76:         alert.setAlertType(type);
77:         alert.setSeverity(severity);
78:         alert.setMessage(message + " @ " + Instant.now());
79:         alertRepository.save(alert);
80:         log.warn("event=alert type={} severity={} message={}", type, severity, message);
81:     }
82: 
83:     private double extractPercentileMs(Timer timer, double percentile) {
84:         ValueAtPercentile[] values = timer.takeSnapshot().percentileValues();
85:         if (values.length == 0) {
86:             return 0;
87:         }
88:         for (ValueAtPercentile candidate : values) {
89:             if (Math.abs(candidate.percentile() - percentile) < 0.001) {
90:                 return toMillis(candidate.value(), timer.baseTimeUnit());
91:             }
92:         }
93:         return toMillis(values[values.length - 1].value(), timer.baseTimeUnit());
94:     }
95: 
96:     static double toMillis(double value, TimeUnit baseUnit) {
97:         return switch (baseUnit) {
98:             case NANOSECONDS -> value / 1_000_000d;
99:             case MICROSECONDS -> value / 1_000d;
100:             case MILLISECONDS -> value;
101:             case SECONDS -> value * 1_000d;
102:             case MINUTES -> value * 60_000d;
103:             case HOURS -> value * 3_600_000d;
104:             case DAYS -> value * 86_400_000d;
105:         };
106:     }
107: }

3: import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
4: import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Autowired;
5: import org.springframework.boot.test.context.SpringBootTest;
6: import org.springframework.scheduling.annotation.Scheduled;
7: import org.springframework.test.context.ActiveProfiles;
8: 
9: import java.time.Instant;
10: import java.util.List;
11: 
12: import static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;
13: 
14: @SpringBootTest
15: @ActiveProfiles("test")
16: class WorkflowEscalationTest {
17: 
18:     @Autowired
19:     private WorkflowTaskRepository repository;
20: 
21:     @Autowired
22:     private WorkflowService workflowService;
23: 
24:     @Test
25:     void evaluateEscalationsMarksOnlyStaleActionableTasks() {
26:         WorkflowTaskEntity staleSubmitted = saveTask(WorkflowStatus.SUBMITTED, Instant.now().minusSeconds(25 * 3600L));
27:         WorkflowTaskEntity staleInReview = saveTask(WorkflowStatus.IN_REVIEW, Instant.now().minusSeconds(26 * 3600L));
28:         WorkflowTaskEntity freshSubmitted = saveTask(WorkflowStatus.SUBMITTED, Instant.now().minusSeconds(2 * 3600L));
29:         WorkflowTaskEntity staleApproved = saveTask(WorkflowStatus.APPROVED, Instant.now().minusSeconds(30 * 3600L));
30: 
31:         List<WorkflowTaskResponse> escalated = workflowService.evaluateEscalations();
32: 
33:         assertThat(escalated)
34:                 .extracting(WorkflowTaskResponse::id)
35:                 .contains(staleSubmitted.getId(), staleInReview.getId())
36:                 .doesNotContain(freshSubmitted.getId(), staleApproved.getId());
37: 
38:         assertThat(repository.findById(staleSubmitted.getId())).get().extracting(WorkflowTaskEntity::isEscalated).isEqualTo(true);
39:         assertThat(repository.findById(staleInReview.getId())).get().extracting(WorkflowTaskEntity::isEscalated).isEqualTo(true);
40:         assertThat(repository.findById(freshSubmitted.getId())).get().extracting(WorkflowTaskEntity::isEscalated).isEqualTo(false);
41:         assertThat(repository.findById(staleApproved.getId())).get().extracting(WorkflowTaskEntity::isEscalated).isEqualTo(false);
42:     }
43: 
44:     @Test
45:     void escalationSchedulerIsConfiguredWithScheduledAnnotation() throws Exception {
46:         Scheduled scheduled = WorkflowEscalationScheduler.class
47:                 .getDeclaredMethod("evaluateStaleTasks")
48:                 .getAnnotation(Scheduled.class);
49: 
50:         assertThat(scheduled).isNotNull();
51:         assertThat(scheduled.fixedDelayString()).isEqualTo("${app.workflow.escalation-check-ms:60000}");
52:     }
53: 
54:     private WorkflowTaskEntity saveTask(WorkflowStatus status, Instant lastActionAt) {
55:         WorkflowTaskEntity task = new WorkflowTaskEntity();
56:         task.setType(WorkflowType.ROUTE_DATA_CHANGE);
57:         task.setMode(WorkflowMode.CONDITIONAL);
58:         task.setStatus(status);
59:         task.setTitle("Escalation test");
60:         task.setPayload("{}");
61:         task.setSubmittedBy("dispatcher_a");
62:         task.setAssignedTo("dispatcher_a");
63:         task.setCollaborators("dispatcher_a");
64:         task.setCurrentStep(1);
65:         task.setTotalSteps(3);
66:         task.setRequiredApprovals(1);
67:         task.setReceivedApprovals(0);
68:         task.setEscalated(false);
69:         task.setLastActionAt(lastActionAt);
70:         return repository.save(task);
71:     }
72: }

# Delivery Acceptance / Project Architecture Review

Project: `/Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91`
Review date: 2026-03-25
Scope reviewed: `/Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack`
Overall judgment: `Partially Pass`

## Verification Summary

- Frontend build was actually executed and succeeded with `cd /Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/frontend && npm install && npm run build`. The configured build entry and output path are defined in `frontend/package.json:5-8` and `frontend/angular.json:11-15`. The generated files exist under `/Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/frontend/dist/mindflow-auth-frontend`.
- Backend runtime and Spring test execution were not executable in this environment because `java` and `mvn` are not installed here. This is an environment restriction, not a project defect. The backend entry and tests are statically confirmable from `backend/Dockerfile:1-20`, `backend/pom.xml:1-79`, `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:27-33`, and `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh:172-284`.
- Docker was not started because the inspection instructions explicitly said not to start Docker-related commands. The documented full-stack path remains reproducible locally from `README.md:9-19`, `docker-compose.yml:1-49`, and `.env.example:1-17`.

## Prioritized Issues

### High

1. Imported/admin-managed data is not connected to passenger search, so the search experience runs on seeded sample data instead of the integration pipeline output.
  - Evidence: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/search/SearchService.java:167-170`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/search/TransitStopBootstrapService.java:18-33`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/integration/DataImportService.java:165-179`
  - Impact: Core business data changes made by admins/import jobs do not propagate to the passenger-facing search feature, weakening the prompt's "bus data integration + version management + search" story.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Persist validated import output into `transit_stops` or a canonical stop catalog and rebuild search from that canonical source instead of the bootstrap sample set.
2. Workflow timeout escalation warnings are manual, not automatic after 24 hours as required.
  - Evidence: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowController.java:85-89`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowService.java:107-120`, `README.md:63-64`
  - Impact: The delivered workflow engine does not autonomously raise timeout alerts; operators only get escalation if someone manually triggers the evaluation endpoint or clicks the UI button.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add a scheduled job that evaluates stale workflow tasks and writes an alert/diagnostic record without manual intervention.

### Medium

1. Notification templates and field dictionaries are CRUD-only; runtime flows do not consume them.
  - Evidence: template CRUD in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/admin/AdminControlService.java:67-95`; hardcoded notification message bodies in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:37-45`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:66-71`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:83-87`; hardcoded import field mapping in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/integration/DataImportService.java:153-157`; dictionary CRUD only in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/admin/AdminControlService.java:98-129`
  - Impact: Admin edits do not materially change notification generation or field mapping behavior, so the "maintain templates / dictionaries" capability is only partially operational.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Resolve notification titles/bodies from `notification_templates` and use `field_dictionaries` during import field normalization/mapping.
2. Sensitivity-level desensitization is not integrated into the real message-center pipeline.
  - Evidence: real inbox records have no sensitivity field in `backend/src/main/resources/schema.sql:36-57`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageEntity.java:23-46`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageQueueEventEntity.java:23-44`; queue masking is generic regex masking in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageQueueService.java:35-44`; sensitivity logic exists only in the separate demo endpoint/service at `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/message/MessageController.java:24-37` and `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/message/MessagePrivacyService.java:9-27`
  - Impact: The prompt requires desensitization "based on sensitivity levels," but the actual inbox path does not carry or enforce sensitivity levels.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add sensitivity metadata to queued/inbox messages and apply `MessagePrivacyService` during message delivery/rendering.
3. Client-side route authorization is absent; browser users can navigate directly to admin/dispatcher pages and rely solely on backend rejection.
  - Evidence: `frontend/src/app/app.routes.ts:10-18`, `frontend/src/main.ts:9-14`, no route guard definitions found under `frontend/src/app/`
  - Impact: This is not a server-side privilege-escalation bug because backend RBAC remains enforced, but it is still a feature-level authorization weakness and a weaker real-product experience.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add route guards for authentication and role-aware navigation hiding.
4. Test coverage is not sufficient to catch the vast majority of core defects.
  - Evidence: no frontend specs under `fullstack/frontend/src`; backend tests listed in `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:10-19` and `backend/src/test/java/**`; `AuthControllerTest` exists at `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/AuthControllerTest.java:1-79` but is omitted from the default unit runner in `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:10-19`; API coverage is narrow in `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh:229-278`
  - Impact: Major flows can regress while the provided test entry points still appear acceptable.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Expand automated tests for message-center ownership, workflow escalation, import HTML/versioning, admin-template application, search edge cases, and frontend component flows.

### Low

1. README startup instructions are present but incomplete because they do not explicitly tell the operator to create `.env` from `.env.example` before Docker Compose startup.
  - Evidence: `README.md:9-29`, required environment values in `.env.example:1-17`, backend startup validation in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/config/SecurityStartupValidator.java:37-75`
  - Impact: First-time operators can hit backend startup failures or blank compose variables even though the repo already provides the example file.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add `cp .env.example .env` to README before `docker-compose up --build`.

## 2. Delivery Completeness

### 2.1 Does the deliverable completely cover the core requirements explicitly stated in the Prompt?

- Conclusion: `Fail`
- Reason (Theoretical Basis): Acceptance clause 2.1 asks for complete coverage of explicit core requirements. This project covers many major features, but several prompt-critical links are incomplete: imported/admin-managed data does not feed search, workflow escalation is manual, runtime templates/dictionaries are not applied, and sensitivity-level masking is not part of the actual inbox pipeline.
- Evidence:

  - Notification preferences/DND implemented: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/notification/NotificationPreferenceService.java:23-63`, `frontend/src/app/settings.component.ts:17-50`

### 2.2 Does the deliverable have a basic 0-to-1 delivery form rather than fragmentary/demo-only code?

Responsibilities are mostly clear and not stacked into a single file.

However, several runtime paths remain hardcoded where extensibility matters most, especially notification template usage, dictionary usage, and the bootstrap search catalog.

## 4. Engineering Details and Professionalism

### 4.1 Do error handling, logging, validation, interface design, and security controls meet basic professional standards?

The score is reduced because client-side route guards are absent, runtime sensitivity-level masking is not integrated, and some operator-facing flows remain manual or CRUD-only.

### 4.2 Does the deliverable resemble a real product/service rather than a teaching demo?

- Conclusion: `Partially Pass`

### 5.1 Does the deliverable accurately understand and respond to the business goals, scenarios, and constraints?

- Conclusion: `Partially Pass`
- Reason (Theoretical Basis): The project understands the three-role scenario, offline local deployment, local auth, in-platform queueing, PostgreSQL persistence, and observability constraints.   - Visit `/`, `/dashboard`, `/dispatcher`, `/messages`, `/admin`, `/observability`
  - Expected result: pages render with consistent spacing, card separation, and interactive feedback without broken assets.

## Security Focus Audit

### Authentication entry point

- Conclusion: `Pass`
- Reason (Theoretical Basis): Authentication is limited to username/password, login is validated, passwords are BCrypt-hashed, and JWT secrets are startup-validated for minimum strength.
- Evidence: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/AuthController.java:20-23`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/AuthService.java:19-26`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/user/UserService.java:28-36`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/config/SecurityStartupValidator.java:37-63`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/config/SecurityConfig.java:90-93`
- Reproducible Verification Method:
  - `cd /Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/backend && mvn test -Dtest=AuthControllerTest`
  - Expected result: valid credentials return JWT; invalid credentials return `401`.

### Route-level authorization

- Conclusion: `Pass`
- Reason (Theoretical Basis): Backend RBAC is clearly enforced at route level for passenger, dispatcher, and admin prefixes.
- Evidence: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/config/SecurityConfig.java:64-70`, `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/RbacAccessTest.java:42-61`
- Reproducible Verification Method:
  - `cd /Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/backend && mvn test -Dtest=RbacAccessTest`
  - Expected result: passenger token gets `403` on dispatcher/admin routes; dispatcher gets `403` on admin routes.

### Object-level authorization

- Conclusion: `Partially Pass`
- Reason (Theoretical Basis): Workflow tasks and messages enforce ownership checks server-side, which is the correct pattern. Coverage is incomplete because only workflow ownership is tested; message ownership is untested.
- Evidence: workflow ownership in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowService.java:122-139` and `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowAuthorizationTest.java:58-115`; message ownership in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageCenterService.java:43-50`
- Reproducible Verification Method:
  - Workflow:
    - `cd /Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/backend && mvn test -Dtest=WorkflowAuthorizationTest`
    - Expected result: second dispatcher gets `403` on another dispatcher's task
  - Message ownership manual API idea:
    - Seed a message as user A, then attempt `/api/passenger/messages-center/{id}/read` as user B
    - Expected result: `403` from `OwnershipDeniedException`

### Feature-level authorization / admin/debug surface

- Conclusion: `Partially Pass`
- Reason (Theoretical Basis): Admin APIs are server-protected, but the frontend does not hide or block direct navigation to sensitive pages via route guards.
- Evidence: admin controller protection `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/admin/AdminControlController.java:21-24`, observability admin protection `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/monitoring/ObservabilityController.java:15-18`, unguarded Angular routes `frontend/src/app/app.routes.ts:10-18`
- Reproducible Verification Method:
  - API:
    - attempt `/api/admin/control/search-weights` without admin token
    - Expected result: `401` or `403`
  - Frontend:
    - browse directly to `/admin` without a valid token
    - Expected result: page shell can still load client-side, but backend requests fail

### Tenant isolation

- Conclusion: `Not Applicable`
- Reason (Theoretical Basis): The prompt describes a local role-based platform but does not define a multi-tenant model, tenant IDs, or cross-tenant boundaries. The relevant isolation boundary in this codebase is per-user resource ownership, not per-tenant partitioning.
- Evidence: schema has no tenant fields in `backend/src/main/resources/schema.sql:1-163`
- Reproducible Verification Method:
  - Inspect schema for tenant identifiers.
  - Expected result: no tenant model is implemented, so tenant-isolation acceptance is out of scope here.

## Unit Tests / API Functional Tests / Log Categorization

### Unit tests

- Conclusion: `Partially Pass`
- Reason (Theoretical Basis): Backend unit/integration-style tests exist, but default execution omits one authentication test class and coverage leaves many risk areas untested.
- Evidence: test inventory in `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:10-19` and `backend/src/test/java/**`; omitted existing test `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/AuthControllerTest.java:1-79`

### API/integration tests

- Conclusion: `Partially Pass`
- Reason (Theoretical Basis): A reproducible bash API suite exists, but it covers only a narrow slice of the system and depends on a running backend. It does not exercise most core business flows.
- Evidence: `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh:172-284`
- Reproducible Verification Method:
  - `cd /Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack && cp .env.example .env && bash API_tests/run_api_tests.sh`
  - Expected result: admin login, invalid login rejection, unauthorized admin access block, user creation, passenger login, booking-event seed, and message-count check.

### Log printing categorization and sensitive-info leakage

- Conclusion: `Pass`
- Reason (Theoretical Basis): Backend logs are structured JSON and include trace IDs; there is no obvious password/token logging in the application code from static review. Sensitive queue/inbox content is masked before rendering, although sensitivity-level integration is incomplete as noted separately.
- Evidence: `backend/src/main/resources/logback-spring.xml:2-11`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/monitoring/TraceIdFilter.java:24-35`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/monitoring/ObservabilityService.java:39-42`, static grep results over backend/frontend source
- Reproducible Verification Method:

  - `rg -n "console\\.log|System\\.out\\.print|printStackTrace|password|Authorization|token" backend/src/main/java backend/src/main/resources frontend/src`
  - Expected result: no application logging of raw passwords or JWTs is found.

## Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)

### Test Overview

- Backend unit/integration-style tests exist under `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/**`.
- Frontend unit tests are effectively missing; `frontend/package.json:5-8` declares `ng test`, but no `*.spec.*` files exist under `frontend/src`.
- API functional tests exist as shell scripts in `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh:1-284`.
- README does provide test entry mention in `README.md:125-129`, but it does not document Java/Maven prerequisites or note that one existing backend test is omitted from the default unit script.
- Environment boundary: tests were not executed here on the backend because `java` and `mvn` are unavailable in this environment.

### Requirement Checklist

1. Local username/password authentication with min 8 chars and salted hashing
2. Route-level authorization for passenger / dispatcher / admin
3. Object-level authorization for workflow tasks and message resources
4. Passenger search by route number / stop name / keyword / pinyin initials, with autocomplete, ranking, and deduplication
5. Notification preferences with toggles, DND, default lead time, and validation
6. Unified message center with reservation success, arrival reminder, missed check-in, queue consumption, and masking
7. Dispatcher workflow engine with conditional/joint/parallel approvals, return, batch action, and 24h timeout escalation
8. Admin runtime controls for templates, sorting weights, and field dictionaries
9. Data import for JSON/HTML, cleaning, missing-value handling, auditing, and stop-structure versioning
10. Observability with health, metrics, trace IDs, queue backlog alerts, and API P95 alerts
11. Core exception semantics: 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409
12. Boundary/risk areas: empty data, filtering, time fields, concurrency/replay, and rollback/data consistency
13. Logs and sensitive-info exposure

### Security Coverage Audit

- Authentication (login/token/session)
  - Conclusion: `Basic Coverage`
  - Evidence: `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/AuthControllerTest.java:51-78`, `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/RbacAccessTest.java:42-61`
  - Boundary: existing login test is not in the default unit runner, and token expiry/disabled-user cases are not covered.
- Route Authorization
  - Conclusion: `Sufficient`
  - Evidence: `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/RbacAccessTest.java:42-61`, enforced rules in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/config/SecurityConfig.java:64-70`
  - Boundary: server-side authorization is solid; frontend route hiding is not tested and not implemented.
- Object-level Authorization
  - Conclusion: `Basic Coverage`
  - Evidence: workflow test `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowAuthorizationTest.java:58-115`; server checks in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowService.java:122-139` and `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageCenterService.java:43-50`
  - Boundary: message ownership has code-level enforcement but no automated test.
- Data Isolation
  - Conclusion: `Partially Covered`
  - Evidence: message ownership and workflow ownership code above; no tenant model in `backend/src/main/resources/schema.sql:1-163`
  - Boundary: per-user isolation exists for some resources, but there is no full regression suite around every user-scoped endpoint.

### Mock / Stub Assessment

- Mock/stub usage is present in unit tests and is acceptable:
  - `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/search/SearchServiceTest.java:22-45` uses mocked repositories/services.
- No evidence was found of a mock payment flow or similar forbidden substitution.
- Risk note:
  - The more important delivery risk is not test mocking itself, but that the runtime search catalog depends on seeded sample data in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/search/TransitStopBootstrapService.java:18-33`.

### Overall Judgment on Whether the Tests Are Sufficient to Catch the Vast Majority of Problems

- Conclusion: `Fail`
- Reason (Judgment Boundary): The current tests do cover some important foundations, especially RBAC basics, workflow ownership, search ranking/dedup happy paths, DND logic, JSON-cleaning happy path, and selected API semantics. However, they do not cover enough of the highest-risk business paths to catch the vast majority of serious defects. Missing areas include message-center ownership, notification preference controller validation, workflow mode semantics and automatic escalation, import HTML/audit/versioning, admin runtime template/dictionary usage, observability alert triggering, frontend behavior, and multiple edge/boundary cases. Because of those gaps, the project could still have severe defects while the provided test suite appears healthy.
- Minimal improvement suggestions by priority:
  1. Add ownership tests for message-center read/delete and add automatic workflow-escalation tests.
  2. Add integration tests for notification preferences, search API, admin template application, and import HTML/audit/versioning.
  3. Add observability alert-trigger tests and a small frontend test suite for login, route gating, and core screens.
  4. Add boundary tests for empty results, time windows, duplicate actions, and malformed inputs.

## Final Acceptance Summary

- The project is substantial and clearly more than a demo. It contains a real full-stack structure, Angular UI, Spring Boot backend, PostgreSQL schema, queueing, admin screens, workflow features, and observability.
- The acceptance result is not a full pass because several prompt-critical behaviors are only partially realized:
  - imported/admin-managed data does not power the passenger search experience
  - workflow timeout warnings are manual instead of automatic
  - admin-maintained templates/dictionaries do not drive runtime behavior
  - sensitivity-level masking is separated from the real inbox pipeline
  - test coverage is not broad enough for the main risk areas
- Acceptance verdict: `Partially Pass`

# Delivery Acceptance / Project Architecture Review

Project: `/Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91`
Review date: 2026-03-25
Scope reviewed: `/Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack`
Overall judgment: `Partially Pass`

## Verification Summary

- Frontend build was actually executed and succeeded with `cd /Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/frontend && npm install && npm run build`. The configured build entry and output path are defined in `frontend/package.json:5-8` and `frontend/angular.json:11-15`. The generated files exist under `/Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/frontend/dist/mindflow-auth-frontend`.
- Backend runtime and Spring test execution were not executable in this environment because `java` and `mvn` are not installed here. This is an environment restriction, not a project defect. The backend entry and tests are statically confirmable from `backend/Dockerfile:1-20`, `backend/pom.xml:1-79`, `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:27-33`, and `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh:172-284`.
- Docker was not started because the inspection instructions explicitly said not to start Docker-related commands. The documented full-stack path remains reproducible locally from `README.md:9-19`, `docker-compose.yml:1-49`, and `.env.example:1-17`.

## Prioritized Issues

### High

1. Imported/admin-managed data is not connected to passenger search, so the search experience runs on seeded sample data instead of the integration pipeline output.
  - Evidence: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/search/SearchService.java:167-170`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/search/TransitStopBootstrapService.java:18-33`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/integration/DataImportService.java:165-179`
  - Impact: Core business data changes made by admins/import jobs do not propagate to the passenger-facing search feature, weakening the prompt's "bus data integration + version management + search" story.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Persist validated import output into `transit_stops` or a canonical stop catalog and rebuild search from that canonical source instead of the bootstrap sample set.
2. Workflow timeout escalation warnings are manual, not automatic after 24 hours as required.
  - Evidence: `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowController.java:85-89`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowService.java:107-120`, `README.md:63-64`
  - Impact: The delivered workflow engine does not autonomously raise timeout alerts; operators only get escalation if someone manually triggers the evaluation endpoint or clicks the UI button.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add a scheduled job that evaluates stale workflow tasks and writes an alert/diagnostic record without manual intervention.

### Medium

1. Notification templates and field dictionaries are CRUD-only; runtime flows do not consume them.
  - Evidence: template CRUD in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/admin/AdminControlService.java:67-95`; hardcoded notification message bodies in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:37-45`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:66-71`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java:83-87`; hardcoded import field mapping in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/integration/DataImportService.java:153-157`; dictionary CRUD only in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/admin/AdminControlService.java:98-129`
  - Impact: Admin edits do not materially change notification generation or field mapping behavior, so the "maintain templates / dictionaries" capability is only partially operational.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Resolve notification titles/bodies from `notification_templates` and use `field_dictionaries` during import field normalization/mapping.
2. Sensitivity-level desensitization is not integrated into the real message-center pipeline.
  - Evidence: real inbox records have no sensitivity field in `backend/src/main/resources/schema.sql:36-57`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageEntity.java:23-46`, `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageQueueEventEntity.java:23-44`; queue masking is generic regex masking in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageQueueService.java:35-44`; sensitivity logic exists only in the separate demo endpoint/service at `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/message/MessageController.java:24-37` and `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/message/MessagePrivacyService.java:9-27`
  - Impact: The prompt requires desensitization "based on sensitivity levels," but the actual inbox path does not carry or enforce sensitivity levels.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add sensitivity metadata to queued/inbox messages and apply `MessagePrivacyService` during message delivery/rendering.
3. Client-side route authorization is absent; browser users can navigate directly to admin/dispatcher pages and rely solely on backend rejection.
  - Evidence: `frontend/src/app/app.routes.ts:10-18`, `frontend/src/main.ts:9-14`, no route guard definitions found under `frontend/src/app/`
  - Impact: This is not a server-side privilege-escalation bug because backend RBAC remains enforced, but it is still a feature-level authorization weakness and a weaker real-product experience.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add route guards for authentication and role-aware navigation hiding.
4. Test coverage is not sufficient to catch the vast majority of core defects.
  - Evidence: no frontend specs under `fullstack/frontend/src`; backend tests listed in `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:10-19` and `backend/src/test/java/**`; `AuthControllerTest` exists at `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/AuthControllerTest.java:1-79` but is omitted from the default unit runner in `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:10-19`; API coverage is narrow in `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh:229-278`
  - Impact: Major flows can regress while the provided test entry points still appear acceptable.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Expand automated tests for message-center ownership, workflow escalation, import HTML/versioning, admin-template application, search edge cases, and frontend component flows.

### Low

1. README startup instructions are present but incomplete because they do not explicitly tell the operator to create `.env` from `.env.example` before Docker Compose startup.
  - Evidence: `README.md:9-29`, required environment values in `.env.example:1-17`, backend startup validation in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/config/SecurityStartupValidator.java:37-75`
  - Impact: First-time operators can hit backend startup failures or blank compose variables even though the repo already provides the example file.
  - Minimal improvement suggestion: Add `cp .env.example .env` to README before `docker-compose up --build`.

## 1. Mandatory Thresholds

### 1.1 Can the deliverable actually run and be verified?

- Conclusion: `Partially Pass`
- Reason (Theoretical Basis): Acceptance requires clear startup instructions and verifiable execution without core-code modification. This repo provides a concrete startup path and test/build scripts, but full runtime verification is only partially achievable in the current environment because backend prerequisites are missing here and the README does not fully document the `.env` preparation step.
- Evidence: `README.md:9-29`, `.env.example:1-17`, `docker-compose.yml:1-49`, `backend/Dockerfile:1-20`, `frontend/package.json:5-8`, `frontend/angular.json:11-15`, `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh:27-33`, `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh:172-284`
- Reproducible Verification Method:
  - Frontend build actually executed here:
    - `cd /Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/frontend`
    - `npm install`
    - `npm run build`
    - Expected result: Angular build succeeds and outputs `/Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/frontend/dist/mindflow-auth-frontend/browser/index.html`
  - Backend in a local machine with Java/Maven:
    - `cd /Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack/backend`
    - `mvn test`
    - Expected result: Spring tests run with H2 according to `backend/src/test/resources/application-test.yml:1-19`
  - Full stack locally:
    - `cd /Users/macbookprom4/devspace/devs-mindflow/TASK-20260318-393C91/fullstack`
    - `cp .env.example .env`
    - `docker-compose up --build`
    - Expected result: services exposed at `http://localhost`, `http://localhost:8080`, `localhost:5432` as documented in `README.md:16-19`

### 1.2 Can it be started without modifying the core code, and does runtime basically match the delivery description?

## Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)

### Test Overview

- Backend unit/integration-style tests exist under `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/**`.
- Frontend unit tests are effectively missing; `frontend/package.json:5-8` declares `ng test`, but no `*.spec.*` files exist under `frontend/src`.

- Environment boundary: tests were not executed here on the backend because `java` and `mvn` are unavailable in this environment.

### Requirement Checklist

1. Local username/password authentication with min 8 chars and salted hashing
2. Route-level authorization for passenger / dispatcher / admin
3. Object-level authorization for workflow tasks and message resources
4. Passenger search by route number / stop name / keyword / pinyin initials, with autocomplete, ranking, and deduplication
5. Notification preferences with toggles, DND, default lead time, and validation
6. Unified message center with reservation success, arrival reminder, missed check-in, queue consumption, and masking
7. Dispatcher workflow engine with conditional/joint/parallel approvals, return, batch action, and 24h timeout escalation
8. Admin runtime controls for templates, sorting weights, and field dictionaries
9. Data import for JSON/HTML, cleaning, missing-value handling, auditing, and stop-structure versioning
10. Observability with health, metrics, trace IDs, queue backlog alerts, and API P95 alerts
11. Core exception semantics: 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409
12. Boundary/risk areas: empty data, filtering, time fields, concurrency/replay, and rollback/data consistency
13. Logs and sensitive-info exposure

| Observability alerts and traceability                                      | `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/monitoring/ObservabilityServiceTest.java:11-21`                                                                                                | only unit-conversion assertions `13-20`                                                                                                                                                                | Insufficient      | No alert-trigger test, no queue backlog test, no trace-ID propagation test                                               | Add tests that create backlog / high timer percentile and assert diagnostics persisted                                                          |
| Core exception semantics 401/404/409                                       | `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/ApiSecuritySemanticsTest.java:52-76`                                                                                                      | `401`, `404`, `409` assertions `54-75`                                                                                                                                                                 | Basic Coverage    | No explicit `400` or `403` in this class; `403` partly covered elsewhere                                                 | Add malformed-request and forbidden-operation tests to centralize API semantics                                                                 |
| API happy-path smoke flow                                                  | `API_tests/run_api_tests.sh:208-278`                                                                                                                                                        | login, create-user, booking-event, count check `208-278`                                                                                                                                               | Basic Coverage    | Does not cover search, settings, workflow actions, admin control, observability alerts, or import pipeline               | Extend bash/API tests to cover at least one full happy path per major module                                                                    |
| Empty data / filtering / time fields / pagination / concurrency / rollback | No dedicated tests found                                                                                                                                                                    | N/A                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Missing           | Major boundary conditions could fail unnoticed                                                                           | Add tests for empty inbox/search, message type filtering, DND boundary times, duplicate workflow actions, and transaction rollback expectations |
| Logs and sensitive-info exposure                                           | No dedicated automated tests; static code review only                                                                                                                                       | structured logs and grep review                                                                                                                                                                        | Unconfirmed       | No test prevents accidental future leakage                                                                               | Add log-sanitization assertions or smoke tests around login/error flows                                                                         |


### Security Coverage Audit

- Authentication (login/token/session)
  - Conclusion: `Basic Coverage`
  - Evidence: `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/AuthControllerTest.java:51-78`, `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/RbacAccessTest.java:42-61`
  - Boundary: existing login test is not in the default unit runner, and token expiry/disabled-user cases are not covered.
- Route Authorization
  - Conclusion: `Sufficient`
  - Evidence: `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/auth/RbacAccessTest.java:42-61`, enforced rules in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/config/SecurityConfig.java:64-70`
  - Boundary: server-side authorization is solid; frontend route hiding is not tested and not implemented.
- Object-level Authorization
  - Conclusion: `Basic Coverage`
  - Evidence: workflow test `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowAuthorizationTest.java:58-115`; server checks in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowService.java:122-139` and `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageCenterService.java:43-50`
  - Boundary: message ownership has code-level enforcement but no automated test.
- Data Isolation
  - Conclusion: `Partially Covered`
  - Evidence: message ownership and workflow ownership code above; no tenant model in `backend/src/main/resources/schema.sql:1-163`
  - Boundary: per-user isolation exists for some resources, but there is no full regression suite around every user-scoped endpoint.

### Mock / Stub Assessment

- Mock/stub usage is present in unit tests and is acceptable:
  - `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/search/SearchServiceTest.java:22-45` uses mocked repositories/services.
- No evidence was found of a mock payment flow or similar forbidden substitution.
- Risk note:
  - The more important delivery risk is not test mocking itself, but that the runtime search catalog depends on seeded sample data in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/search/TransitStopBootstrapService.java:18-33`.

### Overall Judgment on Whether the Tests Are Sufficient to Catch the Vast Majority of Problems

- Conclusion: `Fail`
- Reason (Judgment Boundary): The current tests do cover some important foundations, especially RBAC basics, workflow ownership, search ranking/dedup happy paths, DND logic, JSON-cleaning happy path, and selected API semantics. However, they do not cover enough of the highest-risk business paths to catch the vast majority of serious defects. Missing areas include message-center ownership, notification preference controller validation, workflow mode semantics and automatic escalation, import HTML/audit/versioning, admin runtime template/dictionary usage, observability alert triggering, frontend behavior, and multiple edge/boundary cases. Because of those gaps, the project could still have severe defects while the provided test suite appears healthy.
- Minimal improvement suggestions by priority:
  1. Add ownership tests for message-center read/delete and add automatic workflow-escalation tests.
  2. Add integration tests for notification preferences, search API, admin template application, and import HTML/audit/versioning.
  3. Add observability alert-trigger tests and a small frontend test suite for login, route gating, and core screens.
  4. Add boundary tests for empty results, time windows, duplicate actions, and malformed inputs.

## Final Acceptance Summary

- The project is substantial and clearly more than a demo. It contains a real full-stack structure, Angular UI, Spring Boot backend, PostgreSQL schema, queueing, admin screens, workflow features, and observability.
- The acceptance result is not a full pass because several prompt-critical behaviors are only partially realized:
  - imported/admin-managed data does not power the passenger search experience
  - workflow timeout warnings are manual instead of automatic
  - admin-maintained templates/dictionaries do not drive runtime behavior
  - sensitivity-level masking is separated from the real inbox pipeline
  - test coverage is not broad enough for the main risk areas
- Acceptance verdict: `Partially Pass`

## Gap Remediation Update (2026-03-25)

Implemented remediation for the highest-priority gaps identified in this report.

- `Resolved` Automatic workflow timeout escalation is now scheduled via `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowEscalationScheduler.java`.
- `Resolved` Message-center ownership tests were added for read/delete authorization in `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageCenterAuthorizationTest.java`.
- `Resolved` Notification preference validation tests were added in `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/notification/NotificationSettingsControllerTest.java`.
- `Resolved` Workflow escalation behavior tests were added in `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/workflow/WorkflowEscalationTest.java`.
- `Resolved` Admin templates now drive runtime message generation in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageSchedulerService.java` with coverage in `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/messagecenter/MessageTemplateRuntimeTest.java`.
- `Resolved` Dictionary mappings are now consumed in import normalization and search-catalog write-through in `backend/src/main/java/com/mindflow/security/integration/DataImportService.java` with coverage in `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/integration/DataImportServiceTest.java` and `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/integration/DataIntegrationControllerTest.java`.
- `Resolved` Sensitivity-level metadata is now part of queue/inbox entities (`MessageQueueEventEntity`, `MessageEntity`) and applied during message-center response shaping through `MessagePrivacyService`.
- `Resolved` Observability alert triggering now has dedicated tests in `backend/src/test/java/com/mindflow/security/monitoring/ObservabilityAlertTriggerTest.java`.
- `Resolved` Frontend auth/role route guards were added in `frontend/src/app/auth.guard.ts` and `frontend/src/app/role.guard.ts`, wired in `frontend/src/app/app.routes.ts`.
- `Resolved` Unit test runner now includes expanded suite coverage in `unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh`.

Current backend verification result:

- `Pass` `bash fullstack/unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh` completed with `UNIT_SUMMARY total=15 pass=15 fail=0`.

Residual note:

- Frontend guard unit specs were added (`frontend/src/app/auth.service.spec.ts`, `frontend/src/app/route-guards.spec.ts`) but were not executed in this environment as part of the backend Maven suite.

content.text:
Great newsΓÇöthis pass is now in much better shape and the core gap-remediation increment is shippable.

- I fixed the two failing tests by aligning behavior with runtime details:
  - `WorkflowEscalationTest` now persists `lastActionAt` after entity creation so stale-task escalation assertions are valid with JPA lifecycle hooks.
  - `ObservabilityService` now reads API p95 via `timer.percentile(0.95, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)` first, then falls back to snapshot extraction, avoiding unrealistic conversion artifacts.
- I re-ran the full backend suite via `bash fullstack/unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh` and it now passes: `UNIT_SUMMARY total=15 pass=15 fail=0`.
- I completed and kept the previously requested gap work in place:
  - import-to-search catalog wiring + dictionary application
  - admin template runtime consumption
  - sensitivity metadata through queue/inbox path
  - scheduled workflow escalation
  - ownership/validation/escalation/observability/import/controller tests
  - frontend auth + role guards and guard specs
- I updated the acceptance report with a concrete remediation section and current verification result in `.tmp/self-test_report.md`.

What remains from a ΓÇ£fully closedΓÇ¥ perspective is mostly optional hardening now (not blockers for this increment):
1. Run frontend tests (`ng test --watch=false`) in your environment.
2. Expand API smoke tests in `fullstack/API_tests/run_api_tests.sh` to cover new runtime template/dictionary/search/escalation behavior end-to-end.
3. Add a short release note/changelog entry tied to `.tmp/self-test_report.md` updates.
Have all potential gaps been resolved?

Short answer: **not 100% all potential gaps**, but **all major prompt-critical gaps from the report are now addressed in code**.

- Resolved: importΓåÆsearch wiring, scheduled workflow escalation, admin template/dictionary runtime use, message sensitivity in inbox pipeline, route guards, and the key missing tests.
- Verified: backend expanded suite passes (`15/15`) via `fullstack/unit_tests/run_unit_tests.sh`.
- Still open as residual/non-blocking:
  - Frontend specs were added but not executed in this run.
  - API smoke script is still relatively narrow vs full end-to-end behavior.
  - The earlier low-priority README `.env` setup clarity item was not part of this code pass.

So if you mean ΓÇ£critical acceptance gapsΓÇ¥: **yes, effectively resolved**.  
If you mean ΓÇ£every possible gap including verification/documentation hardeningΓÇ¥: **not yet fully closed**.
Only remaining gap: README still doesn't mention cp .env.example .env before docker-compose up (Low priority).

files.before:
# MindFlow Authentication & Security Foundation

This deliverable contains a local-only full stack implementation under `fullstack/`:

- `backend/`: Spring Boot JWT auth, BCrypt password hashing, RBAC, and data desensitization.
- `frontend/`: Angular login screen and authenticated RBAC dashboard.
- `docker-compose.yml`: PostgreSQL + backend + frontend orchestration.

## Run with Docker Compose

```bash
cd fullstack
docker-compose up --build
```

Services:
- Frontend: `http://localhost`
- Backend API: `http://localhost:8080`
- PostgreSQL: `localhost:5432`

## Bootstrap Admin

- Username: from `BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME` (default `admin`)
- Password: must be provided via `BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD` (no hardcoded default)

Configure via environment variables in `docker-compose.yml`:
- `BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME`
- `BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD`
- `BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_ENABLED`

## Security Notes

- JWT secret must be at least 256-bit base64 value (`JWT_SECRET`).
- Password policy minimum length is 8.
- Role restrictions:
  - Passenger endpoints: PASSENGER / DISPATCHER / ADMIN
  - Dispatcher endpoints: DISPATCHER / ADMIN
  - Admin endpoints: ADMIN only

## Sensitivity Rules

- `LOW`: no masking.
- `MEDIUM`: passenger receives partial masking.
- `HIGH`: passenger and dispatcher receive `[REDACTED]`; admin sees full value.

## Notification Preferences

- Settings page route: `http://localhost/settings`
- Supports:
  - Arrival reminders toggle
  - Reservation success notifications toggle
  - Reminder lead minutes (default `10`)
  - DND time range (`HH:mm` start/end)
- Preferences persist in PostgreSQL user profile and are applied by backend rule logic.

## Dispatcher Workflow Engine

- Dispatcher dashboard route: `http://localhost/dispatcher`
- Features:
  - Create operation tasks by workflow type and mode
  - Approve / reject / return actions
  - Batch approve selected tasks
  - Manual escalation evaluation for tasks unprocessed over 24 hours
- Visual stepper progress per task

## Unified Message Center

- Message center route: `http://localhost/messages`
- Includes:
  - Inbox by type filter (`Reservation Success`, `Arrival Reminder`, `Missed Check-In`)
  - Mark read/unread and delete actions
  - Polling refresh (15s) for near-real-time updates
  - Sensitive value masking for phone/ID-like strings
- Scheduler behavior:
  - Reservation success messages generated from booking events
  - Arrival reminders generated using preference lead time and DND policy
- Missed check-in generated 5 minutes after start time

## Data Integration & Cleaning Engine

- Admin APIs are available under `POST /api/admin/integration/import`.
- Supports ingestion templates:
  - `JSON`: list of objects
  - `HTML`: table rows with stop/address/apartment/area/price columns
- Cleaning output standards:
  - Area unit normalized to `πÄí`
  - Price unit normalized to `yuan/month`
  - Missing fields mapped to `NULL` with source logging
- Persistence and traceability:
  - `import_jobs` for pipeline run status
  - `cleaned_records` for standardized output rows
  - `cleaning_audit_logs` for field-level transformation actions
- `stop_structure_versions` for field-level stop version history

## Administrator Control Panel

- Admin page route: `http://localhost/admin`
- Includes configuration pages for:
  - Notification message templates (CRUD)
  - Search ranking rule weights (relevance/frequency/popularity)
  - Standard field dictionaries (CRUD)
- Updates are persisted in PostgreSQL and applied immediately by backend services.

## Observability & Health Monitoring

- Observability route: `http://localhost/observability`
- Actuator endpoints exposed:
  - `http://localhost:8080/actuator/health`
  - `http://localhost:8080/actuator/metrics`
- Admin diagnostics APIs:
  - `GET /api/admin/observability/snapshot`
  - `GET /api/admin/observability/alerts`
- Alerting conditions:
  - Message queue backlog exceeds threshold (`app.monitoring.queue-backlog-threshold`)
  - API P95 exceeds threshold (`app.monitoring.api-p95-threshold-ms`, default 500ms)

### Local PostgreSQL Backup Strategy

- Backup: `fullstack/backup/backup_postgres.sh`
- Restore: `fullstack/backup/restore_postgres.sh`
- Example:
  - `bash fullstack/backup/backup_postgres.sh ./fullstack/backups`
  - `bash fullstack/backup/restore_postgres.sh ./fullstack/backups/<file>.sql`

## Test Determinism

- `fullstack/run_tests.sh` uses a deterministic test stack bootstrap.
- By default it resets compose resources (`down -v`) before API tests when the stack is not already healthy.
- Override with `TEST_RESET_STACK=false` to keep existing data during local test runs.

- Observability route: `http://localhost/observability`
- Actuator endpoints exposed:
  - `http://localhost:8080/actuator/health`
  - `http://localhost:8080/actuator/metrics`
- Admin diagnostics APIs:
  - `GET /api/admin/observability/snapshot`
  - `GET /api/admin/observability/alerts`
- Alerting conditions:
  - Message queue backlog exceeds threshold (`app.monitoring.queue-backlog-threshold`)
  - API P95 exceeds threshold (`app.monitoring.api-p95-threshold-ms`, default 500ms)

### Local PostgreSQL Backup Strategy

- Backup: `fullstack/backup/backup_postgres.sh`
- Restore: `fullstack/backup/restore_postgres.sh`
- Example:
  - `bash fullstack/backup/backup_postgres.sh ./fullstack/backups`
  - `bash fullstack/backup/restore_postgres.sh ./fullstack/backups/<file>.sql`

## Test Determinism

- `fullstack/run_tests.sh` uses a deterministic test stack bootstrap.
- By default it resets compose resources (`down -v`) before API tests when the stack is not already healthy.
- Override with `TEST_RESET_STACK=false` to keep existing data during local test runs.
